apps.name: The Birthday Card Conductor
apps.description: Tracks every birthday in your life and drafts a personal card in Gmail one week before, with a memory or two pulled from your notes about that person.
apps.howToBuild: Store a small SQLite table via bun:sqlite with name, date, and a notes field for memories. A daily Zo automation runs at 8am, finds birthdays seven days out, and uses the Gmail integration to create a draft addressed to that person. A simple React page on a Zo Space route lets you add or edit people. The draft sits in Gmail so you can edit and send when you want.

apps.name: The School Email Triage
apps.description: Reads incoming school emails, pulls out dates, deadlines, and required forms, and puts a one-page weekly briefing in your inbox every Sunday night.
apps.howToBuild: Use the Gmail integration to fetch messages from school domains over the last seven days. Parse with a small extraction prompt to find dates, money due, and forms to sign. Store results in SQLite. A weekly Zo automation on Sunday at 7pm sends a Gmail digest to yourself with a clean checklist grouped by kid.

apps.name: Vet Reminder Loop
apps.description: A private record for each pet covering shots, weight, and meds, with reminders that arrive two weeks before anything is due.
apps.howToBuild: Build a small Zo Space with a React page per pet and a SQLite table for events. Each visit, paste the vet summary into a text box and the page stores it. A daily automation scans for upcoming due dates and emails you with the pet name, the item, and the clinic phone number. No fancy integrations needed beyond Gmail.

apps.name: The Costco Run Builder
apps.description: A shared family list that learns what you buy on every Costco run and predicts what is probably out by now.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page collects what you bought each trip, with quantity and date. Store rows in SQLite. The page computes a rough days-on-hand average per item and flags anything past its typical lifetime. Share the route privately with your partner so both of you can add items from the road. A weekly Gmail summary on Friday lists likely-low items before weekend errands.

apps.name: The Thank-You Note Coach
apps.description: Log every gift you receive and the coach drafts a personal thank-you note in your voice within 48 hours, ready to send or print.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page captures gift, giver, and a short note about why it mattered. Store in SQLite. A daily automation finds entries older than two days with no draft yet and writes a short note in your style, saved as a Gmail draft addressed to the giver. The page also exports a print-friendly version for paper cards.

apps.name: Family Recipe Vault
apps.description: A private home for the recipes that actually came from people you know, with the story attached to each one.
apps.howToBuild: Use a Zo Space with a React form to add recipes including a photo, ingredients, steps, and a story field. Store in SQLite and store images as space assets via update_space_asset. Add a search route that queries by ingredient or person. A weekly automation picks one recipe and emails it to you on Saturday morning as a cook-this-week suggestion.

apps.name: The Subscription Cemetery
apps.description: Scans your Gmail for recurring receipts and shows every subscription you are paying for, including the ones you forgot about.
apps.howToBuild: Use the Gmail integration to search receipts and invoices over the last 12 months. A parser identifies sender, amount, and cadence, then stores rows in SQLite. A Zo Space page shows total monthly burn, last charge date, and a one-click link to the merchant cancel page. A monthly automation emails you a hit list of anything you have not used.

apps.name: The Babysitter Briefing
apps.description: One page that has every single thing a sitter needs: bedtimes, allergies, emergency contacts, where the snacks live, and the wifi password.
apps.howToBuild: Build a single Zo Space page protected with a private route. Store the data in SQLite so it is editable by you, and render a clean print-ready layout with phone numbers as tap-to-call links. Add a public share link that uses a long random token in the URL so you can text the link to a sitter without exposing it to the world.

apps.name: Hand-Me-Down Sizing Tracker
apps.description: Tracks what fits each kid right now and what is sitting in the bin waiting to grow into, so you stop buying clothes that already exist in your basement.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page with a SQLite table for items by kid, size, season, and location (closet, bin A, bin B). Photo upload uses update_space_asset. A search page filters by what is currently in storage at the next size up. A seasonal automation emails you in April and October with the list of items ready to graduate from the bin.

apps.name: The Holiday Card Roster
apps.description: Keeps every address current and tracks who you sent to last year and who you skipped, so December stops being a panic.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space form for adding people and addresses, stored in SQLite. Each year the page lets you tick who got a card. A November automation emails you the list of stale addresses (people whose info is older than 18 months) so you can verify before printing labels. Export the active list as CSV for whatever label tool you use.

apps.name: Carpool Schedule Roulette
apps.description: A fair, predictable rotation for the parent group, with a polite reminder text to whoever is up each morning.
apps.howToBuild: Track families and weekdays in a SQLite table. A Zo Space page shows the next two weeks of assignments and lets people swap. A daily automation at 6am sends an email to the driver of the day. If a swap happens, it updates the rotation so the long-term balance stays even across families.

apps.name: Permission Slip Catcher
apps.description: A single home for every school form that needs a signature, with a record of when it was signed and returned.
apps.howToBuild: Use a Zo Space page that lets you upload a photo or PDF of the slip, with fields for due date, kid, and status. Store metadata in SQLite and the file via update_space_asset. A daily automation flags anything within 48 hours of due with no completed status and emails you. Done items move to an archive view so the active page stays short.

apps.name: The Pantry Expiration Watch
apps.description: Tells you on Sunday what is about to expire in the pantry and suggests two meals you can actually make from it this week.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page lets you scan items in (manual entry with name and date is fine). Store rows in SQLite. A Sunday automation finds items within 14 days of expiration and writes a short Gmail digest with simple meal suggestions based on what overlaps. Add an undo so you can mark items as eaten or tossed.

apps.name: RSVP Wrangler
apps.description: Manages the messy reality of who is coming to a backyard birthday or potluck, including the maybes and the plus-ones.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page hosts an event with a guest list table in SQLite and a public RSVP route that uses a token in the URL. Guests pick yes, no, or maybe and add who they are bringing. An automation sends you a Saturday morning headcount the week of the event, plus a polite Gmail nudge to anyone still showing as no response.

apps.name: The Doctor Visit Recap
apps.description: After every appointment, dictate what was said and the recap saves a clean summary you can search later when you forget what the specialist actually told you.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page with an audio recorder element and an upload route. Use the transcribe_audio tool to convert the recording to text, then store both the audio asset and the transcript in SQLite tagged by date and provider. A search page lets you query past visits. A monthly automation emails a recap of all appointments that month so nothing slips.

apps.name: Heirloom Story Recorder
apps.description: Sends your grandparent a single question every Sunday and saves their voicemail or text reply as a chapter in a family book.
apps.howToBuild: Keep a list of prompts (where did you grow up, what did your father do, etc) in SQLite. A weekly automation picks one and texts it via send_sms_to_user to the contact. Replies come back and you paste them into the Zo Space page, or use transcribe_audio for voicemails saved as assets. The page renders all entries chronologically as a long-form book ready for print.

apps.name: Lost and Found Closet
apps.description: A searchable map of where things live in your house so the next time you need the good scissors you do not lose 20 minutes.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page with a SQLite table for item name, location, photo, and last-used date. Tag items with rooms and containers. Add a search route that handles fuzzy matches. Optional: a weekly automation emails you a list of items you have not touched in over a year as a gentle declutter prompt.

apps.name: Pet Medication Schedule
apps.description: Multiple pets, multiple meds, multiple times a day, all reduced to one page that says exactly what to give whom right now.
apps.howToBuild: SQLite tables for pets and prescriptions with dose, frequency, and start date. A Zo Space page renders today's schedule as a tap-to-complete checklist. A daily automation at the first dose time sends an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the morning rundown. A history view shows missed doses so you can flag patterns for the vet.

apps.name: The Wedding Vendor War Room
apps.description: Tracks every quote, deposit, and follow-up question across florists, caterers, photographers, and the venue, so nothing falls through.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space with React pages per vendor category and a SQLite store for contacts, quotes, deposits paid, and open questions. Upload contracts via update_space_asset. A weekly automation emails you the list of vendors who have not responded in over five business days with suggested follow-up text. A countdown page shows days to wedding.

apps.name: The Plant Watering Calendar
apps.description: Every plant gets its own watering rhythm and you get a single morning email telling you which three to water today.
apps.howToBuild: SQLite table per plant with location, last watered, and target interval. A Zo Space page shows today's list with a single tap to mark watered. A daily automation at 7am sends a short Gmail with the three highest-priority plants. Seasonal toggle adjusts intervals for winter when most houseplants need less.

apps.name: Bills Due This Week Glance
apps.description: A Sunday-evening email listing every bill hitting in the next seven days with the amount, the autopay status, and what is actually negotiable.
apps.howToBuild: Use the Gmail integration to scan billing emails over the last 60 days and extract amounts and due dates into SQLite. A Zo Space page lets you mark which bills are on autopay and add a note like 'call to negotiate' on items like internet. A Sunday automation at 6pm sends you the next-seven-days view by email.

apps.name: The Senior Parent Bill Concierge
apps.description: Helps an adult child quietly monitor an aging parent's bills without taking over their accounts, by tracking due dates and unusual charges.
apps.howToBuild: The parent forwards billing emails to a dedicated Gmail label. The Gmail integration pulls those messages, a parser extracts amount and merchant, and SQLite stores history. A Zo Space page shows a calendar of due dates and a flagged list of charges that are 50% higher than the rolling average. Weekly automation emails the adult child a short summary.

apps.name: The Gas Receipt Mileage Log
apps.description: Forward gas station receipts to a Gmail label and your mileage log builds itself, ready to hand to a tax preparer in April.
apps.howToBuild: Use the Gmail integration to read messages with the gas-receipts label. A parser extracts date, station, and amount. Store rows in SQLite alongside the vehicle. A simple React page lets you tag each entry as personal or business. A monthly automation emails a CSV-ready summary. End-of-year, export the full log as CSV from the page.

apps.name: The Webhook Replay Inspector
apps.description: Catches every webhook your service receives, stores the raw payload, and lets you replay any one of them into your dev environment with a single click.
apps.howToBuild: Create a Hono API route on Zo Space at /api/hook/:source that accepts POSTs, validates a shared secret, and stores headers and body in SQLite via bun:sqlite. A React page lists recent payloads with filters by source and status. A replay route POSTs the stored body to a configurable target URL. Add bearer auth on the dashboard route.

apps.name: Indie Hacker MRR Mirror
apps.description: A personal monthly recurring revenue board across multiple Stripe accounts and side projects, with the truth in one place instead of three dashboards.
apps.howToBuild: Use the Stripe integration (and bearer-protected webhook routes) to ingest subscription events into SQLite, one table per project. A React dashboard route computes MRR, churn, and net new per month. A monthly automation on the first sends a Gmail recap with the deltas. Support multiple Stripe keys via env vars so each project stays separate.

apps.name: Cold Outbound Permission Checker
apps.description: Before you send a cold pitch, paste the list and it flags addresses that are likely opt-out, GDPR-restricted, or your own customers already.
apps.howToBuild: Build a Zo Space API route that takes a CSV of emails, joins against your existing customer list and unsubscribe list stored in SQLite, and runs simple heuristics on domains (gov, edu, eu-tld) for higher-care flags. A React page shows the audit with green, yellow, and red rows and a download of the cleaned list. No external APIs required.

apps.name: The Stale Pull Request Bot
apps.description: Walks your repos every Monday and lists PRs that have been open too long, with the reviewer, the days-open count, and a suggested polite nudge.
apps.howToBuild: Use the GitHub integration via the github skill (gh CLI) to fetch open PRs across your repos. Store snapshots in SQLite so you can see week-over-week movement. A weekly automation on Monday at 9am sends a Gmail digest of PRs older than seven days, sorted by author. A React page lets you mark some PRs as intentionally on hold.

apps.name: SaaS Trial Expiry Pager
apps.description: Knows when every tool you have tried is about to convert to paid and pages you 48 hours before so the decision is not a surprise charge.
apps.howToBuild: Use the Gmail integration to scan for trial confirmation emails and 'your trial ends' notices. Parse out the service and the expiration date, store in SQLite. A daily automation finds anything within 72 hours and sends both an email and an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the service name and the cancel link if you have stored one.

apps.name: The Bug Bounty Inbox
apps.description: A dedicated triage queue for incoming security reports, with severity tagging, deduplication against past reports, and a canned reply that does not sound dismissive.
apps.howToBuild: Forward security@ mail to a Gmail label. The Gmail integration ingests new messages into SQLite. A small classifier prompt tags severity (info, low, med, high, critical). A Zo Space page renders the queue with filters and a one-click 'mark duplicate' that links to the prior report. A weekly automation emails a backlog summary so nothing rots.

apps.name: Indie Launch Day Runbook
apps.description: A timed checklist for the 24 hours of a Product Hunt or Show HN launch, with prompts that fire at the right hour and a log of what worked.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page with a SQLite-backed checklist seeded from a template (post at 12:01am PT, ping mailing list at 8am, etc). Each task has an hour offset from launch. A scheduled automation steps through the list and sends an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the next action. After launch, the page shows time-to-completion per task so the next launch is sharper.

apps.name: Stripe Refund Postmortem
apps.description: Every refund gets a one-question follow-up email and the answers turn into a tiny dataset of why people actually left.
apps.howToBuild: Use a Stripe webhook handler on a Hono route to listen for charge.refunded events. The handler stores the refund in SQLite and, 24 hours later via a delayed automation, sends a Gmail message asking 'mind sharing what did not work?' with a short reply link back to your space. Replies append to the same record. A dashboard groups reasons into clusters.

apps.name: The OSS Sponsor Wall Generator
apps.description: Builds a clean sponsors page for an open source project, refreshed nightly from GitHub Sponsors, with tiers and a hall of past sponsors.
apps.howToBuild: Use the gh CLI via the github skill to pull current sponsors and past sponsors weekly into SQLite. A React route renders tiers as a public page with avatars (cached as space assets via update_space_asset). A nightly automation refreshes the data and rebuilds the page. Add an opt-out flag so sponsors who request privacy do not appear.

apps.name: UTM Source Cleaner
apps.description: Paste a messy list of campaign links and it normalizes the UTMs, flags collisions, and gives back a single clean schema you can paste into your tracking sheet.
apps.howToBuild: A Hono API route takes a list of URLs and parses utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, utm_content. Rules in SQLite store your canonical values (e.g. 'fb' becomes 'facebook'). A React page shows before-and-after with conflict highlights. Export the cleaned list as CSV. No external service required, everything runs in the space.

apps.name: The Founder Update Cadence
apps.description: Drafts your monthly investor update from the actual events of the month (Stripe revenue, GitHub merges, calendar meetings) so you start from a real first draft.
apps.howToBuild: Pull Stripe MRR for the month, gh CLI merge counts per repo, and Google Calendar events tagged as customer or investor. A monthly automation on the 28th writes a draft markdown update into a Gmail draft addressed to your investor list, with placeholders for narrative sections. You edit, then send. Past updates archive in SQLite so the next month references prior promises.

apps.name: Client Brief Diff
apps.description: Compares this week's client brief against the original signed scope and highlights every line item that has quietly grown.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space page lets you paste the original SOW and every subsequent brief. Store versions in SQLite. A diff route compares text and tags added scope (new deliverables, new platforms, expanded counts). A React page shows side-by-side with the new items highlighted. Export the diff as a PDF-ready page you can send back as 'here is what changed, here is the new estimate'.

apps.name: The Manual Status Page
apps.description: When something is broken and you do not have time to log into a status service, post incidents directly from SMS and the public page updates within seconds.
apps.howToBuild: A Hono route accepts incident posts from your phone via send_sms_to_user reply handling, gated by a known number and a keyword. Posts store to SQLite with status (investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved). A public React page renders the timeline at /status with semantic styling. An RSS feed route gives subscribers a programmatic feed.

apps.name: SEO Title Length Auditor
apps.description: Crawls a sitemap, pulls every page title and meta description, and flags the ones that will be truncated in Google results before you publish more.
apps.howToBuild: A Hono route accepts a sitemap URL, fetches each page server-side, and parses out title and meta description. Store in SQLite with character counts. A React page shows a sortable table with the truncation threshold visualized. A weekly automation re-crawls and emails a Gmail diff of what changed. Add a CSV export for handing to whoever owns content.

apps.name: The Demo Call Clip Hub
apps.description: After every sales or demo call, drop the recording in and it transcribes, finds the three highest-energy moments, and saves short clips you can paste into a sales doc.
apps.howToBuild: A Zo Space upload route stores the recording as a space asset. transcribe_audio (or transcribe_video) produces a transcript with speaker segments. A scorer ranks 30-second windows by question density and excitement words. The page exports the top three windows as timestamp links and saves a text summary in SQLite. A weekly automation emails a clip digest.

apps.name: The Sunday Reset Inbox
apps.description: A weekly Sunday-night view that pulls your unread Gmail, surfaces what actually needs a reply, and quietly archives newsletters and receipts.
apps.howToBuild: Connect Gmail and write a Hono API route that reads unread messages from the last seven days. Score each thread by sender history and whether it contains a question mark or a deadline phrase. Render a React page that shows three buckets (reply, read, archive) and lets you click to bulk-archive. Schedule a Sunday 6pm automation to email yourself the link.

apps.name: Birthday Heads-Up Calendar
apps.description: Pulls birthdays from your Google Contacts and emails you a card-and-gift reminder ten days before, not the morning of.
apps.howToBuild: Read contacts via Google People API through the Gmail connection or a CSV import. Store birthdays in SQLite with bun:sqlite. A daily cron automation queries the database for birthdays exactly ten days out and sends an email with the person's name, last gift you recorded, and a link to a one-field form for jotting this year's idea.

apps.name: The Subscription Audit
apps.description: Scans your Gmail for recurring charges, groups them by vendor, and tells you which ones you have not opened, watched, or logged into this year.
apps.howToBuild: Search Gmail for receipt-shaped messages from common billing senders (Stripe, PayPal, Apple, Google). Parse amount and date, store in SQLite, and roll up by vendor. A React page lists vendors with monthly cost, annualized cost, and a checkbox for 'still using'. Export the unchecked rows as a cancel-this-week list.

apps.name: Kid Permission Slip Tracker
apps.description: Catches school emails that ask for a signature or a check, and parks them on one page with due dates and a sign-status flag.
apps.howToBuild: Filter Gmail for messages from the school domain that contain words like 'permission', 'sign', or 'return by'. Extract the date with a simple regex pass and store in SQLite. Render a React table sorted by deadline with three states (received, signed, returned). A morning automation texts you the day before anything is due.

apps.name: Pantry Expiration Watcher
apps.description: A barcode-free pantry log where you type what you bought and when, and it nags you before things expire instead of after.
apps.howToBuild: Build a Hono API route that accepts a one-line entry (item, bought date, shelf life). Store rows in SQLite. A React form on the homepage adds items, and a daily automation emails the three items closest to expiry. No fancy CV, just honest manual entry that takes ten seconds.

apps.name: The Returns Window Tracker
apps.description: Reads Amazon and other receipt emails, computes the last day you can return each item, and shows you only what is still in window.
apps.howToBuild: Query Gmail for shipping confirmations from common retailers. Parse order date and item title, then add a configurable return window per retailer (30 days Amazon, etc) stored in SQLite. A React page lists items with days remaining. A weekly automation sends a 'returns expiring this week' email.

apps.name: Holiday Card List Keeper
apps.description: One page that holds the mailing addresses you actually use once a year, sourced from Gmail confirmations and edited by hand.
apps.howToBuild: Pull shipping addresses from past Gmail order confirmations sent to friends and family, then dedupe by name. Store in SQLite and expose a React table with edit-in-place. Add a CSV export so you can hand it to a print service. The list lives quietly all year and reactivates in November.

apps.name: Doctor Visit Recap Sheet
apps.description: A short structured form you fill out right after an appointment so you do not forget what the doctor actually said by Thursday.
apps.howToBuild: Build a React form with five fields (date, provider, what they said, what to do, follow-up by). Save to SQLite via a Hono API route. The homepage shows a reverse-chron list with a search box. A monthly automation emails you any follow-ups that are now overdue.

apps.name: Pet Vaccine Calendar
apps.description: Keeps your dog or cat's shots, flea meds, and vet visits on a single page with the next-due date front and center.
apps.howToBuild: A simple SQLite schema with pet, treatment, last given, interval. React form adds entries. A daily automation checks for anything due in the next 30 days and emails a reminder. Add a print view so you can hand the page to a boarder or new vet.

apps.name: Grocery Price Memory
apps.description: You snap or type the prices on your receipt; it remembers what a dozen eggs cost six months ago at the same store.
apps.howToBuild: Build a fast React entry form (store, item, price, date) backed by SQLite. Add a search-by-item view that plots the last twelve months of prices for that item across stores. No OCR needed for v1, just trust manual entry. Later you can add receipt parsing from emailed digital receipts.

apps.name: Family Group Chat Digest
apps.description: Paste in a long iMessage or WhatsApp export and get a one-screen digest of what the family actually decided this week.
apps.howToBuild: A React page with a big textarea and a 'summarize' button. Submit to a Hono API route that calls the Zo /zo/ask endpoint with a structured prompt asking for decisions, open questions, and dates mentioned. Render the result as three short lists. Optional: save digests to SQLite by week.

apps.name: The Lent Item Tracker
apps.description: Logs the books, tools, and casserole dishes you lent to friends so you can stop pretending you remember where your good drill went.
apps.howToBuild: A React form with item, lent to, lent date, expected back. Store in SQLite. List view sorted by how long it has been out. A monthly automation emails 'still out' items with a one-click 'mark returned' link that hits a Hono API route.

apps.name: Weekend Plan Suggester
apps.description: Checks the local weather forecast and your Google Calendar, then proposes three plausible Saturday plans by Thursday night.
apps.howToBuild: A Thursday automation calls a free weather API for your zip code, reads Saturday's blocks from Google Calendar, then asks Zo's /zo/ask for three plan ideas given the weather, free hours, and a list of your preferences stored in a small text file. Email the result with links to maps or tickets.

apps.name: Mileage Logger for Tax
apps.description: A two-tap log for work-related driving so you have an actual number at tax time instead of a guess.
apps.howToBuild: Mobile-friendly React form with one big 'Start trip' button that records start time, then 'End trip' for end time and ending odometer. Store rows in SQLite with purpose and client. A yearly automation exports a CSV to your Gmail in January. Add an editable rate so you can apply the current IRS mileage rate.

apps.name: Wedding RSVP Wrangler
apps.description: Pulls your invite emails into one list, lets you mark yes or no, and emails the host on your behalf with a personal note.
apps.howToBuild: Filter Gmail for messages with subject lines containing 'wedding' or 'invitation'. Store extracted dates and senders in SQLite. A React page shows each invite with a yes/no/maybe toggle and a 'compose reply' button that opens a templated note. Send via the Gmail integration on confirmation.

apps.name: The Thank-You Note Queue
apps.description: After a birthday, wedding, or funeral, this holds the list of who gave what so you actually send the cards before next year.
apps.howToBuild: React form for event, person, gift, address. Store in SQLite. The main page shows a queue with three states (not sent, written, mailed). A weekly automation emails you the oldest unsent five with a one-line draft for each, generated via /zo/ask using the gift detail as context.

apps.name: Heirloom Item Catalog
apps.description: A private archive for the dozen objects in your house that have a story, so the story does not die with the person who told it.
apps.howToBuild: React form lets you upload a photo via update_space_asset, then type provenance, year, and the story. Store metadata in SQLite. The main page is a grid view with click-through detail pages. Keep the route private. Add an export-to-PDF button using pandoc on the server for family-day printing.

apps.name: Bill Due Calendar
apps.description: Reads your Gmail for bill notification emails and assembles them on a single page in due-date order with the dollar amount.
apps.howToBuild: Search Gmail for messages from known billers (electric, water, credit cards) using a vendor list in SQLite. Regex out amount and due date. Render a React calendar view. A daily automation emails you the morning before anything is due. No bank login required, just inbox.

apps.name: The Take-Out Memory
apps.description: Logs what you ordered, where, and whether it was actually good, so the next Friday night decision takes thirty seconds.
apps.howToBuild: Pull DoorDash and Uber Eats receipts from Gmail and parse restaurant and items. Store in SQLite. Add a one-tap rating column on the React page. The homepage shows top-rated places sorted by 'haven't ordered in 30+ days' so you rotate instead of repeating the same Thai twice a week.

apps.name: Babysitter Briefing Sheet
apps.description: One printable page for the sitter with bedtimes, allergies, contacts, and the wifi password, kept current so you stop retyping it.
apps.howToBuild: Store kids, schedule blocks, emergency contacts, and house notes in SQLite. A React print-friendly page renders them. Add a 'tonight only' section editable from your phone for the current sitter (special instructions, where you'll be). Print or email it via Gmail with one click before you leave.

apps.name: Library Due Date Companion
apps.description: Logs library checkouts and texts you two days before each one is due, because the library email always lands in spam.
apps.howToBuild: Forward library checkout emails to a special Gmail label, or enter items manually. Parse title and due date into SQLite. A daily automation finds due-in-two-days rows and sends an SMS via the Zo SMS tool. Bonus: a renew-status field you toggle when you've extended.

apps.name: Houseplant Watering Rotation
apps.description: A rotation page for your houseplants that learns the interval per plant instead of nagging you about the cactus every Tuesday.
apps.howToBuild: React form with plant name, default interval, last watered. SQLite stores rows. A daily automation looks at last_watered + interval and emails today's list. Add a 'watered today' button that updates the timestamp via a Hono API route. Optional photo per plant via update_space_asset.

apps.name: Staging URL Inventory
apps.description: A live list of every preview and staging URL across your projects with last-deploy time and last-known status code.
apps.howToBuild: Store environments (project, url, owner) in SQLite. A Hono API route polls each url with fetch and records status + response time. Render a React dashboard with red/green dots. Schedule a five-minute automation to refresh. Optional: post to a Slack-compatible webhook when a previously-green env starts failing.

apps.name: Deploy Window Tracker
apps.description: A timeline of every production deploy with the commit hash, who shipped it, and what broke in the next 48 hours.
apps.howToBuild: Receive deploy events via a Hono webhook from your CI (GitHub Actions, Vercel). Store in SQLite. A React timeline shows deploys with an 'incident link' field you fill in by hand when something blows up. After 90 days, compute a 'risk by author / day of week / hour' rollup. Quietly informative, no blame.

apps.name: The Postmortem Drafting Pad
apps.description: A structured form for writing blameless incident reports so they actually get finished instead of dying in a Notion draft.
apps.howToBuild: React form with the standard sections (summary, timeline, root cause, what went well, what didn't, action items). Save drafts to SQLite. A 'publish' button writes the final markdown to /home/workspace and optionally pushes it to a GitHub repo via the gh CLI. Include a checklist of follow-ups with owners.

apps.name: Cron Job Witness
apps.description: A heartbeat catcher for your scheduled jobs that complains when one of them quietly stops running.
apps.howToBuild: Expose a Hono API endpoint that accepts pings with a job name and status. Store every ping in SQLite. For each registered job, compute expected interval and flag jobs whose last ping is older than 1.5x the interval. A daily automation emails the silent jobs. Wire your real cron jobs to curl the endpoint at the end of each run.

apps.name: Bug Reproduction Library
apps.description: A searchable archive of customer bug reports with the exact steps, environment details, and the eventual fix linked to a commit.
apps.howToBuild: React form for ticket id, description, steps, env, status. Store in SQLite. Add a GitHub commit field that fetches commit info via the gh CLI when filled in. The list view is a search box plus filter chips. A weekly automation summarizes unresolved bugs older than 14 days and emails the on-call.

apps.name: Cohort Retention Plotter
apps.description: Drop a CSV of signups and activity events and get a classic retention triangle without booting up a notebook.
apps.howToBuild: React file upload posts a CSV to a Hono API route that parses with Bun's built-in CSV-friendly streaming. Compute weekly cohorts in SQLite using window functions. Render the triangle as a styled HTML table with conditional shading. Save uploaded datasets so you can revisit them. No external analytics service.

apps.name: Stripe Refund Reason Tracker
apps.description: Pulls refunds from your Stripe account, lets you tag each one with a reason, and shows the top reasons by month.
apps.howToBuild: A Hono API route calls the Stripe API for recent refunds using the STRIPE_SECRET_KEY secret. Store id, amount, customer, date in SQLite. React page shows untagged refunds with a quick-tag UI (broken, duplicate, fraud, mind change, support failure). Charts roll up tags by month so patterns surface.

apps.name: Onboarding Friction Funnel
apps.description: A simple funnel view of your signup flow built from your own event log so you can see exactly where new users quit.
apps.howToBuild: Define a small step schema (signup, verified, first_action, activated) and write events to SQLite from your app via a Hono API route. The dashboard reads counts per step and renders a bar funnel in React. Add a date range picker. Export drop-off cohorts as CSV so you can email them follow-up sequences.

apps.name: Lead Source Reconciler
apps.description: Joins UTM-tagged signups from your site against actual Stripe customers so you know which campaigns paid the rent.
apps.howToBuild: Capture UTM params on signup via a Hono API route and store in SQLite. Pull Stripe customers and their first charges via the Stripe API. Match on email. Render a React table grouped by source and campaign with signups, paid customers, MRR. A weekly automation emails the top and bottom three sources.

apps.name: PR Review Queue Aging
apps.description: Shows every open pull request in your repos sorted by how long it has been waiting on a human, not on CI.
apps.howToBuild: Use the gh CLI to list open PRs across configured repos. Store snapshots in SQLite with timestamps. A React page sorts by 'time waiting on a reviewer', computed by ignoring time spent on CI runs or draft state. Daily automation posts the worst offenders to your email or a Discord channel via the Zo Discord tool.

apps.name: SaaS Renewal Calendar
apps.description: One calendar of every vendor renewal with the price, the notice window, and a 'still using this?' check before auto-renew hits.
apps.howToBuild: Manually seed a SQLite table or import from Gmail receipt scrape (vendor, amount, renewal date, notice days). Render a React calendar plus a list view sorted by renewal date. A daily automation checks for renewals inside the notice window and emails a one-click 'cancel' or 'keep' decision page with a notes field.

apps.name: Dependency Heatmap
apps.description: Reads your package.json files across projects and highlights duplicate versions, abandoned packages, and the ones you keep meaning to remove.
apps.howToBuild: Scan /home/workspace for package.json files via Bun's fs APIs. For each dependency, hit the npm registry for last publish date and downloads. Store in SQLite. Render a heatmap-style React table where rows are packages and columns are projects, color-coded by version drift and staleness.

apps.name: The Decision Log Mirror
apps.description: Paste a meeting transcript and it pulls out the decisions, owners, and dates as a clean append-only log you can search later.
apps.howToBuild: React form with a transcript textarea and a project picker. POST to a Hono API route that asks /zo/ask for a structured extraction (decision, owner, date, rationale). Append rows to SQLite. The main view is a filterable timeline. Export to markdown so it lives next to your project docs in /home/workspace.

apps.name: Marketing Asset Approval Queue
apps.description: A lightweight queue where draft posts, ads, and emails wait for one named approver before they ship, with a clear timestamp trail.
apps.howToBuild: React form to submit an asset (title, channel, body, draft link). Store in SQLite with state machine columns (draft, in_review, approved, scheduled, shipped). Email the approver via Gmail with an inline approve link that hits a token-protected Hono API route. Show audit history per asset on a detail page.

apps.name: Indie Founder Burn Mirror
apps.description: A weekly mirror of your real expense and MRR numbers, with a runway count in months, so you stop guessing how scared you should be.
apps.howToBuild: Pull Stripe MRR via the Stripe API. Maintain a small SQLite table of recurring expenses (hosting, tools, salary draw) you edit by hand. A Hono API route computes monthly burn and runway from cash on hand (one field you keep current). React dashboard shows a single big number and a 12-week sparkline. Weekly automation emails it.

spaces.name: Fridge Door Note
spaces.description: A single sticky note for the household. Whoever opens it last writes what is gone. A text fires when nothing has been written in three days.

spaces.name: The Sunday Reset Page
spaces.description: A Sunday-only checklist that wipes every week. Laundry, meal prep, calendar peek, and one small joy. Hidden the other six days.

spaces.name: Babysitter Briefing Sheet
spaces.description: A printable one-pager with bedtime, allergies, the neighbor's number, and where the snacks live. Share by link, nothing to install.

spaces.name: Pet Medication Calendar
spaces.description: Tracks every pill and dose for each pet. Marks today, warns about refills, keeps a history the vet can actually read.

spaces.name: The Did I Take My Meds Log
spaces.description: One tap per pill. Shows a green dot for today and a grid for the month, so you stop second-guessing at 9pm.

spaces.name: Recipe Doubler
spaces.description: Paste a recipe, pick a multiplier, get clean fractions back instead of 1.3333 cups. Handles metric too.

spaces.name: The Lunch Decision Wheel
spaces.description: A spinning wheel of the six places you actually order from. Removes the option you picked last time so you stop landing on the same thing.

spaces.name: Dog Walk Log
spaces.description: Hit one button when you leave, another when you get back. Builds a weekly map of walk minutes and gentle nudges when the streak slips.

spaces.name: The Tuesday Dinner Rotation
spaces.description: A six-week rotation of dinners the family eats without arguing. Shows tonight, prints the grocery list, retires anything that flopped.

spaces.name: Permission Slip Stash
spaces.description: A drop box for school forms. Snap a photo, get a clean PDF and a due date, then a text the morning it has to go back.
spaces.keyTech: React, Hono, Tesseract OCR, Twilio API

spaces.name: The Drawer Inventory
spaces.description: One photo per drawer, tagged with what is inside. Search passport and find out it lives in the kitchen junk drawer.

spaces.name: School Pickup Roster
spaces.description: Today's pickup driver, today's pickup time, today's after-school activity. One screen the whole family can read at a glance.

spaces.name: The Grocery List That Remembers Last Week
spaces.description: Type the usual and it pre-fills with what you bought last Saturday. Cross items off on your phone, add new ones, never lose the list.

spaces.name: Birthday Wall for People You Forget
spaces.description: Pulls birthdays from Google Contacts and shows the next four. Texts you the morning of, with a one-line draft message to copy.
spaces.keyTech: React, Hono, Google People API, Cron

spaces.name: The Where Did I Park Tracker
spaces.description: One big button. Tap it when you get out of the car. Tap Find Car to see the spot on a map and the photo you took.

spaces.name: Houseplant Mood Diary
spaces.description: For each plant: last watered, current mood (drooping, thriving, dramatic), and a short log of what you tried. Less of a tracker, more of a journal.

spaces.name: Library Book Due Date Page
spaces.description: Scan a barcode, set the due date, get a reminder two days before. Shows which books match which kid's reading level.
spaces.keyTech: React, Hono, ZXing barcode scanner, Cron

spaces.name: The Spice Rack Audit
spaces.description: A grid of every spice you own with a still smells like cumin toggle. Flags anything older than a year so you stop sprinkling sawdust.

spaces.name: Allowance Tracker for Kids
spaces.description: One row per kid, one column per chore. The kids check off their own boxes, payday math happens on Sunday automatically.

spaces.name: Holiday Card Address Book
spaces.description: Last year's list, who sent one back, who you forgot, and a print-ready label sheet for this year. Quiet from January to October.

spaces.name: Car Maintenance Memory
spaces.description: Last oil change, last tire rotation, last weird noise. The page that finally answers when did we get the brakes done.

spaces.name: Subscriptions I Forgot About
spaces.description: A wall of every recurring charge pulled from your inbox. Sorts by haven't opened in 90 days so cancelling becomes a Saturday hobby.

spaces.name: The Refrigerator Calendar
spaces.description: A full-screen week view designed to live on a kitchen tablet. Pulls from Google Calendar, big text, no chrome, readable from across the room.

spaces.name: The OAuth Token Funeral
spaces.description: Lists every API token stashed in env vars, sorted by how soon it expires. Sends a heads-up two weeks before each one dies.

spaces.name: PR Idle Time Wall
spaces.description: Every open pull request across your repos, sorted by hours since last activity. The ones past three days turn red and start blinking.

spaces.name: Build Time Leaderboard
spaces.description: Tracks how long each CI run takes. Charts the trend per branch and shouts when a build slips past the ten minute line.
spaces.keyTech: React, Hono, GitHub Actions API, Recharts

spaces.name: The Dependency Bump Diary
spaces.description: Every package update across your projects in one feed. Major bumps get a yellow tag, breaking changes get a red one, you decide what to read.
spaces.keyTech: React, Tailwind, npm registry API, GitHub API

spaces.name: Customer Quote Wall
spaces.description: A wall of nice things customers said, pulled from your support inbox. Click any to copy the quote with attribution into a slide deck.

spaces.name: The Pricing Variant Switcher
spaces.description: Serves a different pricing page to half your visitors, logs conversion events, and tells you when one variant is statistically ahead.
spaces.keyTech: React, Hono, SQLite, cookies, Stripe API

spaces.name: Database Backup Status Board
spaces.description: Green dot when last night's backup ran, red dot when it did not. Charts backup size trend so you notice when something stopped being included.
spaces.keyTech: React, Hono, SQLite, S3 API, Cron

spaces.name: The Failed Webhook Bin
spaces.description: Catches webhooks your app dropped, stores the full payload, and gives you a one-click retry button. Stripe, GitHub, Linear, anyone.

spaces.name: Stripe MRR Mirror
spaces.description: Today's MRR, this month's change, the five newest customers, the three most recent churns. Loads in under a second from a cached snapshot.
spaces.keyTech: React, Hono, Stripe API, SQLite, Cron

spaces.name: The Beta Tester Invite Door
spaces.description: A waitlist page that hands out one invite code per day. Tracks who actually showed up and quietly demotes the ones who never logged in.

spaces.name: API Rate Limit Dashboard
spaces.description: One row per third-party API you depend on. Shows current usage against the cap, projected exhaustion time, and which endpoint is the loudest.

spaces.name: Open Source Star History Chart
spaces.description: Paste a repo URL, get a clean star-over-time chart. Compare up to three repos side by side without the noisy hosted versions.

spaces.name: The Postmortem Template Wall
spaces.description: One folder of post-incident docs in a consistent template. Tagged by service, severity, and root cause so the same fire stays put out.

spaces.name: Indie Launch Day Page
spaces.description: A single page that flips from soon to live the moment your countdown hits zero. Pulls early signups from a Stripe waitlist link.

spaces.name: The Sentry Error Triage Board
spaces.description: Pulls open errors from your error tracker, groups them by file, and lets you assign owners with one click. Quiet when nothing is on fire.

spaces.name: Sunday Week-Ahead Glance
spaces.description: A single calm page that pulls next week from Google Calendar and shows it as one quiet column. No notifications, no badges, just the shape of the week.
spaces.keyTech: React, Hono, Google Calendar API, Tailwind

spaces.name: Fridge Whiteboard
spaces.description: A shared family note that anyone in the household can edit from their phone. Big text, autosaves, no login fuss.

spaces.name: The Pantry Ledger
spaces.description: A tap-to-decrement list of what's actually in the kitchen. Click a can of beans, it goes down by one. Empty items float to a shopping section.

spaces.name: Inbox Triage Mailbox
spaces.description: Pulls the last twenty Gmail threads and sorts them into Reply, Read, Trash piles with one keystroke per email. Apply changes in a batch at the end.

spaces.name: The Refund Tracker
spaces.description: A small ledger for refunds you are owed but have not received. Reminds you on day seven and day fourteen so nothing quietly disappears.

spaces.name: Birthday Radar
spaces.description: Reads contacts and shows the next eight birthdays as a horizon line. Tap one to draft a Gmail with a starting sentence already written.

spaces.name: The Grandma Letter Page
spaces.description: A page that nudges you once a month to write one paragraph to someone who matters. Saves the letters as a private archive you can print later.

spaces.name: Closet Inventory Page
spaces.description: Snap a photo, add a one-word tag, and your wardrobe lives in a scrollable grid. Filter by color when you cannot decide what to wear.

spaces.name: The Subscription Wall
spaces.description: Every recurring charge you pay, listed by monthly cost with renewal dates. A red dot appears next to anything you have not opened in 60 days.

spaces.name: Tonight's One Thing
spaces.description: A single input every morning that asks for the one thing that would make tonight feel okay. Saves a quiet history of those answers.

spaces.name: Doctor Visit Notes
spaces.description: A simple form you fill in the parking lot after an appointment. Date, doctor, three things they said, what you decided. Easy to read later.

spaces.name: The Borrowed-Things Ledger
spaces.description: Track who has your stuff and what you have of theirs. Two columns, no shame, gentle reminders after a month.

spaces.name: Family Recipe Card Wall
spaces.description: A grid of handwritten-feeling recipe cards from your own kitchen. Click one to flip it over for the method. No ads, no life story above the recipe.

spaces.name: The Quiet Receipt Page
spaces.description: A page that swallows photos of paper receipts, names them by date and merchant, and quietly stores them as a tax-time archive.

spaces.name: Permission Slip Generator
spaces.description: Pick a kid, pick a field trip, get a printable PDF ready to sign. Lives quietly in case the next teacher asks for the same form.

spaces.name: Pet Medication Schedule
spaces.description: A small grid showing which dog gets which pill on which day, with a check-off you can hit from a phone in the kitchen.

spaces.name: The Permission-to-Rest Page
spaces.description: A page that does almost nothing. It says, in serious typography, that you are allowed to rest today, and shows the date so you remember when you needed it.

spaces.name: Funeral RSVP Page
spaces.description: A respectful single page for a memorial: time, place, parking, who to call, and a yes-or-no form. No emojis, no banner, no theme music.

spaces.name: The Lawn-Mowing Memory
spaces.description: One tap when you mow the lawn. The page shows how many days since last cut and quietly predicts the next one based on the season.

spaces.name: The Soft Goodbye Page
spaces.description: A page you give a friend who is leaving the company or the city. They scribble notes, you read them later. Slow, written, not a Slack thread.

spaces.name: The 'Did I Eat' Tracker
spaces.description: Three buttons for breakfast, lunch, dinner. That is it. A calendar fills in over a month so you can see the pattern without judgement.

spaces.name: The Power Outage Page
spaces.description: A bare-bones page for a household to point at during a storm. Phone numbers, who to call first, where the flashlights live, when the last outage was.

spaces.name: Pre-Commit Hook Inspector
spaces.description: Paste a repo URL, see every Git pre-commit hook and which files would block a commit. Helpful when joining a new codebase.

spaces.name: The Stale PR Wall
spaces.description: Every PR you opened that has been open more than seven days, sorted by silence. Click one to draft a polite nudge in your voice.

spaces.name: Stripe Refund Reason Histogram
spaces.description: Reads the last 90 days of Stripe refunds and groups the reason field into a bar chart. Tells you what is actually breaking, not what you think is.

spaces.name: The Bus-Factor Page
spaces.description: Looks at the last 90 days of GitHub commits per repo and flags any file only one person has touched. The quiet pre-mortem for a small team.

spaces.name: Cold DM Composer
spaces.description: Paste a person's bio and a one-sentence ask. Returns three short opener drafts in your own voice, none of them starting with 'Hope you are doing well'.

spaces.name: Linear Issue Smell Test
spaces.description: POST a Linear issue ID and get back a short critique: is the title specific, is there a clear acceptance criterion, is the scope honest. Returns JSON.
spaces.keyTech: Hono, Linear API, Zo Ask API

spaces.name: API Latency Honesty Page
spaces.description: Pings your own endpoints from five public locations every fifteen minutes and shows the p50, p95, and worst day. The numbers your status page hides.

spaces.name: Indie Hacker Pricing A/B Page
spaces.description: Two pricing pages at one route, served at random, tracking which one leads to a Stripe checkout. No third-party script, no banner, no cookie wall.

spaces.name: The Changelog Comparison Page
spaces.description: Paste two GitHub release tags and see the merged PR titles between them, grouped by author and label. Useful before writing a real changelog.

spaces.name: The Cron Drift Reporter
spaces.description: Tracks the actual run times of your scheduled jobs against their cron expressions. Tells you when a midnight job is silently firing at 12:11.

spaces.name: The Open Graph Auditor
spaces.description: Paste a domain, get a grid of every public URL's Open Graph card rendered exactly as Slack, X, and LinkedIn would render it. Spot the broken ones at a glance.

spaces.name: The Postmortem Folder Page
spaces.description: An index of every incident you have written up, with the date, the duration, the one-sentence cause, and the lesson. A reading room, not a database.

spaces.name: The Friday Demo Page
spaces.description: Drop a loom link, a GIF, or a paragraph by Friday at noon. The page shows the team's week as five small tiles. No standup, no slide deck.

spaces.name: Webhook Replay Console
spaces.description: Catches incoming webhooks, stores the raw payload, and lets you replay any of them against a local endpoint with one click. The debugger Stripe never gave you.

spaces.name: Repo Onboarding Map
spaces.description: Point it at a GitHub repo and get a one-screen map: top folders, most-edited files this quarter, the people to ask about each one. For your first day.

automations.name: Trash Day Eve Reminder
automations.prompt: It is Sunday evening, the night before trash and recycling pickup. Send the user an SMS using send_sms_to_user that says: 'Trash + recycling out tonight. Don't forget the kitchen bag.' Keep it under 140 characters. Do not include any other content.
automations.expectedOutput: A short SMS Sunday evening reminding the user to take the trash and recycling to the curb.
automations.customization: Change the day, the pickup items mentioned, or the delivery channel to email or Telegram.

automations.name: The Friday Inbox Sweep
automations.prompt: It is Friday afternoon. Use use_app_gmail with tool gmail-find-email to list unread messages from the past 7 days in the primary inbox. Group them into three buckets: (1) Needs a reply before Monday, (2) Read later this weekend, (3) Probably ignore. For each item include sender, subject, and a one sentence reason. Then send the user an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Friday Inbox Sweep' with the three buckets as markdown sections. Do not send any replies on the user's behalf.
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday afternoon email sorting the week's unread Gmail into reply, read later, and ignore piles.
automations.customization: Adjust the lookback window, the bucket definitions, or the day and time the sweep runs.

automations.name: Sunday Pantry Snapshot
automations.prompt: Read the markdown file at /home/workspace/Home/pantry.md which the user updates by hand. Parse the checklist style entries (lines beginning with - [ ] or - [x]). Build a list of items marked low or out. Send the user an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Sunday Pantry Snapshot' listing those items as a clean shopping list, plus any items that have been low for more than two weeks based on dates in the file. Keep the email under 200 words.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday morning email with a tidy shopping list of low or out pantry items pulled from a local markdown file.
automations.customization: Change the file path, the checklist format the agent parses, or the threshold for flagging long standing low items.

automations.name: School Pickup Weather Window
automations.prompt: Use web_search to fetch the hourly forecast for the user's city for the window 2pm to 4pm local time. Identify rain, snow, lightning, or wind above 25 mph. If any of those are present, send an SMS via send_sms_to_user that names the condition and the exact hour it lands, plus a one line suggestion like 'grab the rain jacket' or 'park closer than usual'. If the window is clear, do nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A conditional SMS sent only when the school pickup window has bad weather, with the exact hour and a practical suggestion.
automations.customization: Change the pickup window hours, the city, or the weather thresholds that trigger an alert.

automations.name: Pharmacy Refill Lead Time
automations.prompt: Read the user's medication list at /home/workspace/Health/meds.md. Each entry has a name, daily dose, and pill count last updated date. For each medication, estimate days of supply remaining. If any medication will run out in 5 days or fewer, send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user listing those medications and how many days are left. Do not send anything if every medication has more than 5 days left.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning SMS only when one or more prescriptions are within 5 days of running out, listing exactly which ones.
automations.customization: Change the lead time threshold, the file path, or the delivery channel.

automations.name: The 10pm Tomorrow Briefing
automations.prompt: It is 10pm. Use use_app_google_calendar to list tomorrow's events on the primary calendar. Use web_search to get tomorrow's weather and any travel advisories for the user's city. Build a Telegram message with three sections: 'On the calendar' (events with times), 'Weather' (high, low, precipitation chance, notable conditions), 'Heads up' (one or two practical notes such as 'leave 15 minutes earlier, snow likely on the pass'). Send via send_telegram_message. Keep under 1000 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: A nightly Telegram message summarizing tomorrow's calendar, weather, and any practical heads up notes.
automations.customization: Change the time, the calendar, the city, or the three section structure.

automations.name: Library Book Due Buzzer
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/library.md, a markdown table of borrowed books with title, due date, and renewable flag. Find any book due within 2 days. If there are any, send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user listing them with title and due date, and note which ones can still be renewed. If none, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning SMS only when library books are due within 2 days, including which ones can be renewed.
automations.customization: Change the lead time, the file format, or the channel.

automations.name: Birthday Card Mail-By Date
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/birthdays.csv (columns: name, birthday, address_known). For any birthday landing in the next 10 to 14 days where address_known is yes, treat this as the mail-by week. Send the user an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Mail a card this week' listing each name, their birthday, and the suggested mail-by date (birthday minus 3 days). Skip anyone without an address on file.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email naming everyone whose birthday card needs to be in the mailbox this week.
automations.customization: Change the lookahead window, the source file, or the buffer days for postal travel time.

automations.name: The Hydration Nag
automations.prompt: Send a single short SMS via send_sms_to_user telling the user to drink water. Rotate between five lines so it doesn't feel robotic: 'Water break.', 'Pour a glass.', 'Top off.', 'Drink up.', 'Refill the bottle.' Pick one based on the current hour modulo 5. Under 40 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: A short rotating water reminder SMS three times during the workday.
automations.customization: Change the times, the rotation of lines, or the channel.

automations.name: Vet Visit Countdown for the Dog
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Pets/lucky.md which lists upcoming vet appointments, vaccine due dates, and flea/tick treatment schedule. Find anything due in the next 21 days. Send a Monday email via send_email_to_user titled 'Lucky vet countdown' with a short list: item, due date, days away. If nothing is in the next 21 days, send a one line note: 'Nothing on Lucky's schedule in the next three weeks.'
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email with the dog's next three weeks of vet related items, or a short all clear if nothing is pending.
automations.customization: Change the lookahead window, the file path, or the pet's name.

automations.name: The Sunday Meal Map
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/meals/this-week.md which lists planned meals for the coming week as a markdown table (day, meal, key ingredients). Also read /home/workspace/Home/pantry.md to check which key ingredients are already on hand. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Sunday Meal Map' with two sections: the week's plan as a table and a deduplicated shopping list of only the ingredients NOT already in the pantry.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday email showing the week's meal plan and a shopping list filtered against what is already in the pantry.
automations.customization: Change the file paths, the week start day, or the table format.

automations.name: Tuesday Pollen Heads-Up
automations.prompt: Use web_search to look up the pollen forecast for the user's city for the next 5 days. Identify the day with the highest count and the day with the lowest. Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the format: 'Pollen this week: peak [DAY] ([level]), easiest [DAY] ([level]).' Keep under 100 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: A Tuesday morning SMS naming the worst and easiest pollen days of the week ahead.
automations.customization: Change the city, the day the briefing runs, or the channel.

automations.name: Receipts to Folder Sorter
automations.prompt: Use use_app_gmail with gmail-find-email to find emails received today with subjects containing 'receipt', 'order confirmation', 'invoice', or 'payment'. For each match, save the email body and any PDF attachment to /home/workspace/Finance/receipts/YYYY/MM/sender-subject.pdf or .md. Create folders as needed with run_bash_command. Do not send any notification. Just sort and save.
automations.expectedOutput: A silent daily job that files the day's receipt and invoice emails into a clean year/month folder structure.
automations.customization: Change the subject keywords, the destination folder layout, or run it weekly instead of daily.

automations.name: The Coupon Expiry Pinger
automations.prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search the inbox and Promotions tab for the past 60 days for messages containing 'coupon', 'expires', '% off', or 'code'. Extract any expiration date you can find in the body. Build a list of coupons that expire within the next 14 days. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Coupons about to expire' with a table of store, code, discount, expires on. Skip duplicates.
automations.expectedOutput: A Saturday email listing coupons from your inbox that expire in the next two weeks, with codes and discount amounts.
automations.customization: Change the lookahead window, the search keywords, or the day of the week.

automations.name: Town Council Meeting Watch
automations.prompt: Use web_search to find the user's town or city council agenda for any meeting scheduled this week. If found, use read_webpage to grab the agenda text. Build a plain English summary: meeting date and time, top 5 agenda items, any item flagged for public comment. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Council meeting this week'. If no meeting is scheduled, send a one line note saying so.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email with this week's council agenda summarized in plain English, or a short note that no meeting is scheduled.
automations.customization: Change the city, the day the watch runs, or how many agenda items to summarize.

automations.name: County Road Closure Alert
automations.prompt: Use web_search to check the state or county DOT page for road closures and chain restrictions affecting the user's county for today. If there are any active closures or chain laws on the roads the user typically travels, send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user naming the road and the restriction. If nothing is active, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A weekday morning SMS only when there are active road closures or chain restrictions in the user's county.
automations.customization: Change the roads watched, the county, or what counts as alert worthy.

automations.name: The Camper Propane Check
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/camper-log.md which has weekly entries of propane tank level (in percent or pounds), date, and notes. Find the most recent entry. If the level is at or below 30 percent, send an SMS via send_sms_to_user saying so and recommending a refill before the next cold snap. If above 30 percent, send a short status SMS like 'Propane fine, [level] as of [date].'
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday evening SMS with current propane status pulled from a hand maintained log, plus a refill nudge if it is low.
automations.customization: Change the threshold percent, the log file location, or the channel.

automations.name: Phone Photo Backup Sanity Check
automations.prompt: Use use_app_google_drive to count files in the 'Google Photos' or 'Camera Uploads' backup folder modified in the past 7 days. Compare to the prior week's count if you can find a saved counts file at /home/workspace/Home/photo-backup-counts.csv. Append this week's count to that file. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Photo backup check' with this week's count, last week's count, and a clear flag if this week is more than 50 percent below last week (possible sync issue).
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday night email comparing this week's photo backup count to last week, flagging suspicious drops.
automations.customization: Change the folder watched, the drop threshold, or how many weeks of history to keep.

automations.name: Birthday Gift Idea Stash
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/birthdays.csv and /home/workspace/Contacts/gift-ideas.md. For any birthday landing in the next 60 days, pull any gift idea notes the user has saved about that person from gift-ideas.md. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Gift ideas to start sourcing' that lists each upcoming birthday with the captured ideas. If no ideas exist for someone, suggest one open question the user could text them to gather hints.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email pairing upcoming birthdays with already saved gift ideas, and a question to ask when there are none.
automations.customization: Change the lookahead window, the source files, or the cadence.

automations.name: Plant Watering Pulse
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/plants.md which lists each plant with its watering interval in days and the date last watered. Compute days since last watered for each plant. Build a Telegram message with three sections: 'Water today', 'Water in 1-2 days', 'Fine for now'. Send via send_telegram_message. Keep under 800 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: A Telegram message twice a week sorting plants into water now, water soon, or fine.
automations.customization: Change the days the pulse runs, the file format, or add a 'mist only' category for tropicals.

automations.name: The Laundry Forecast
automations.prompt: Use web_search to fetch the Saturday and Sunday forecast for the user's city. Pick the best day for line drying based on: sun, low humidity, low wind that is not zero, no rain. Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the format: 'Best laundry day: [Sat or Sun]. [reason in 6 words].'
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday evening SMS naming the better weekend day to do laundry, with a six word reason.
automations.customization: Change the city, the day window, or the factors that count toward 'best'.

automations.name: Daily Step Goal Heckle
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Health/steps.csv (date, steps). Get today's steps. If today is below 5000, send a needling SMS via send_sms_to_user: pick a random heckle from ['Couch is winning today.', '5k or it didn\'t happen.', 'Lucky needs a walk anyway.', 'You owe yourself ten minutes.']. If above 5000, send a one line 'good day' style note. If no entry for today, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A 7pm SMS that either heckles the user toward more steps or briefly acknowledges a good day.
automations.customization: Change the threshold, the heckle lines, or the time it fires.

automations.name: Sunset Walk Window
automations.prompt: Use web_search to look up today's sunset time for the user's city, plus the next two hours of weather. If conditions during the hour before sunset are walkable (no rain, no thunderstorm, wind under 25 mph, temp above 25F), send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with: 'Sunset walk window: [start time] to [sunset time]. [temp]F.' If unwalkable, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: An afternoon SMS naming the exact hour before sunset that is good for a walk, only when conditions are clear.
automations.customization: Change the city, the weather thresholds, or how far before sunset to start the window.

automations.name: The Mystery Charge Detector
automations.prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search for bank and card transaction emails from the past 7 days (filters: 'transaction', 'charged', 'receipt', 'authorized'). Extract merchant name and amount. Group by merchant. Flag any merchant the user has NOT seen in the prior 90 days of similar emails (a new payee). Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Mystery charges this week' listing only the new or unusual ones with date and amount. Skip everything familiar.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email listing only charges from merchants that haven't appeared in the user's email in the past 90 days.
automations.customization: Change the comparison window, the keywords searched, or the threshold for what counts as new.

automations.name: The Family Group Chat Recap
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/family-notes.md where the user dumps quick lines about family conversations during the week. Summarize what each named family member shared, mentioned, or needed help with. Then suggest three small things the user could do or say in the coming week to follow up. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Family recap and 3 follow ups'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday night email summarizing the week's family touchpoints with three specific follow up ideas.
automations.customization: Change the source file, the family members tracked, or the number of follow up suggestions.

automations.name: Morning Stretch Trigger
automations.prompt: Send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user with one of these five stretch prompts rotated by day of week: Mon 'Hip flexors, two minutes.', Tue 'Cat cow, ten reps.', Wed 'Shoulder rolls, both ways.', Thu 'Calf wall stretch, 30s each.', Fri 'Standing forward fold, breathe.' Keep under 60 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: A weekday morning SMS giving a single named stretch to do before the day starts.
automations.customization: Change the stretch prompts, the time, or the days it fires.

automations.name: Wikipedia Article of the Day Pocket
automations.prompt: Use read_webpage to fetch https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and extract the 'Today's featured article' section. Write a tight 4 sentence summary in plain English, plus three follow up topics the user might want to read about next. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Wiki Pocket: [article title]'. Include the link to the full article at the bottom.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning email with a 4 sentence summary of Wikipedia's featured article plus three related topics to explore.
automations.customization: Change the source (e.g. Featured Picture, On This Day), the summary length, or the channel.

automations.name: PR Stale Reviewer Nudge
automations.prompt: Run `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,author,createdAt,reviewDecision,reviewRequests --repo $(cat /home/workspace/Projects/active-repo.txt)` via run_bash_command. Parse the JSON. Identify any PR open more than 3 days with no review decision and at least one pending review request. Build a markdown list: PR title, number, days open, reviewer handles. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Stale PRs needing review' to the user. Do not nudge reviewers directly. The user pushes them.
automations.expectedOutput: A twice weekly email listing open PRs that have been waiting on a named reviewer for more than three days.
automations.customization: Change the staleness threshold, the repo file path, or which repos to scan.

automations.name: Stripe MRR Drift Alarm
automations.prompt: Run `stripe subscriptions list --limit 100 --status active -d 'data[].items'` via run_bash_command and compute current MRR in USD by summing per period unit amounts normalized to monthly. Append today's date and MRR to /home/workspace/Finance/mrr-history.csv using create_or_rewrite_file. Compare today's MRR to yesterday's. If the absolute change is over 5 percent in either direction, send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the prior value, today's value, percent change, and direction. Otherwise send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning SMS only when daily MRR drifts more than 5 percent up or down, with the numbers and direction.
automations.customization: Change the alert threshold, the currency, or the history file location.

automations.name: The Postmortem Drafter
automations.prompt: Check /home/workspace/Projects/incidents/inbox/ for any new file added today (a one line incident note from the user). If found, for each note: read it, then grep_search related log files under /home/workspace/Projects/logs/ for the time window mentioned. Draft a postmortem at /home/workspace/Projects/incidents/drafts/YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md using the template: Summary, Timeline, Detection, Impact, Root Cause (best guess), Open Questions, Action Items. Mark the file [DRAFT]. Do not delete the original inbox note.
automations.expectedOutput: A dated postmortem draft file written into the incidents/drafts folder whenever a new incident note appears.
automations.customization: Change the inbox path, the template sections, or which log files to grep.

automations.name: Vercel Build Failure Digest
automations.prompt: Run `vercel ls --json` and `vercel inspect <deployment> --json` via run_bash_command across the user's project list at /home/workspace/Projects/vercel-projects.txt. Collect any deployments in the past 24 hours with state ERROR or CANCELED. For each, capture project, branch, commit hash, error type, and the last 15 lines of the build log. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Vercel failures last 24h' with a section per failure. If no failures, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning email only when Vercel had failed or canceled deployments in the past day, with project, branch, and tail of the build log.
automations.customization: Change the project list, the lookback window, or what counts as a noteworthy failure.

automations.name: NPM Audit Auto-Triage
automations.prompt: For each repo path listed in /home/workspace/Projects/repos.txt, cd into it and run `npm audit --json` via run_bash_command. Parse vulnerabilities. Group by severity (critical, high, moderate, low). For each critical and high, identify whether a fix is available without breaking changes. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'NPM audit Monday' with a per repo table: severity, package, fix available, breaking. Skip dev only dependencies unless severity is critical.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email listing critical and high npm vulnerabilities across the user's repos, with which ones have non breaking fixes.
automations.customization: Change the repos scanned, the severity threshold, or whether to include dev dependencies.

automations.name: The Cold Email Reply Salvager
automations.prompt: Use use_app_gmail to find cold outbound emails the user sent in the past 14 to 30 days (sent folder, no reply in thread, not in a known existing client conversation). Build a list of recipients who never replied. For each, draft a 3 sentence soft follow up that does not nag, asks a different question than the original, and offers an easy out. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Salvage these cold replies' with the list, original subject, and draft follow up text. Do not send anything to the prospects yourself.
automations.expectedOutput: A Wednesday email with drafted soft follow ups for cold outreach that has gone silent 2 to 4 weeks ago.
automations.customization: Change the silence window, the follow up tone, or how many to draft per week.

automations.name: SEO Rank Slip Watch
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Marketing/tracked-queries.csv (columns: query, target_url). For each row, use web_search to find the current Google rank of target_url for that query (top 30 results only). Append today's results to /home/workspace/Marketing/rank-history.csv. Compare against the rank one week ago. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Rank movement this week' with two tables: 'Slipped 3+ positions' and 'Climbed 3+ positions'. Skip queries with no change.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email showing which tracked queries climbed or slipped at least three positions in Google over the past week.
automations.customization: Change the tracked queries, the movement threshold, or the comparison window.

automations.name: Indie Hacker Launch Radar
automations.prompt: Use read_webpage to fetch the Indie Hackers 'Milestones' page and the Product Hunt daily 'Top' page. Pull the top 10 of each. For each item, capture name, one line description, and link. Filter to launches that match the user's interest tags in /home/workspace/Marketing/interest-tags.txt (e.g. 'ai writing', 'developer tools', 'productivity'). Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Launch radar today' with two short sections. Cap at 6 items total.
automations.expectedOutput: A daily email with up to six indie launches matching the user's interest tags from Indie Hackers and Product Hunt.
automations.customization: Change the interest tags, the sources scanned, or the daily cap.

automations.name: Linear Cycle Burn Pulse
automations.prompt: Use use_app_linear to list issues in the currently active cycle for the team in /home/workspace/Projects/linear-team.txt. Count total issues, completed, in progress, and not started. Compute days remaining in the cycle. Estimate completion rate at current pace (completed per day so far) and forecast end of cycle. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Cycle pulse: [team] [cycle name]' with the numbers and a one paragraph honest read: on track, tight, or slipping.
automations.expectedOutput: A mid week email with a numeric snapshot of the active Linear cycle and an honest on-track/tight/slipping verdict.
automations.customization: Change the team, the day the pulse runs, or the forecasting math.

automations.name: Product Hunt Competitor Sniff
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Marketing/competitor-keywords.txt (lines of keywords like 'ai writing assistant', 'content studio'). Use read_webpage on https://www.producthunt.com/ to grab today's launches. For each launch, check if any of its name, tagline, or topics contain any of the keywords (case insensitive). If matches exist, send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'PH competitors today' listing each match with link and tagline. If none, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A daily email only when a Product Hunt launch matches the user's competitor keyword list, with links and taglines.
