automations.customization: Change the keyword file, the matching logic, or whether to include the previous day's launches too.

automations.name: The Lighthouse Score Weekly
automations.prompt: For each URL in /home/workspace/Projects/lighthouse-urls.txt run `npx lighthouse <url> --output=json --quiet --chrome-flags='--headless'` via run_bash_command and parse the four category scores (performance, accessibility, best practices, SEO). Append a row to /home/workspace/Projects/lighthouse-history.csv with date and scores. Compare against the prior Monday. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Lighthouse Monday' with a table of current scores and the delta from last week. Flag any category that dropped 5 or more points.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email with current Lighthouse scores for tracked URLs and any category that lost 5+ points week over week.
automations.customization: Change the URL list, the drop threshold, or the audit categories tracked.

automations.name: Postgres Slow Query Roundup
automations.prompt: Run a query via psql in run_bash_command against the database in env var DATABASE_URL: SELECT query, calls, mean_exec_time, total_exec_time FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 10. Format the results into a markdown table truncating query text to 120 chars. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Top 10 slow queries this week' with the table plus a one line suggestion per query (missing index, large scan, N+1 shape, etc.) where you can guess. Reset pg_stat_statements after sending only if /home/workspace/Projects/pgstat-reset.flag is present.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email with the database's ten slowest queries ranked by total time, plus a guess at why each is slow.
automations.customization: Change the ranking metric, the row cap, or whether to auto reset stats each week.

automations.name: The Cron Job Health Audit
automations.prompt: List all automations in /home/workspace/Projects/automations/ and any cron definitions in /home/workspace/Projects/*/crontab files. For each, capture name, schedule, last successful run timestamp (from any log file alongside it). Identify (a) automations that have not run in 30+ days, (b) automations with overlapping schedules, (c) automations with no log file at all. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Monthly cron audit' with three sections, one per category. Suggest delete, fix, or investigate per item.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email auditing every scheduled job for staleness, schedule overlap, or missing logs.
automations.customization: Change the staleness threshold, the directories scanned, or the audit categories.

automations.name: AWS Cost Anomaly Catcher
automations.prompt: Run `aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=$(date -d 'yesterday' +%F),End=$(date +%F) --granularity DAILY --metrics UnblendedCost --group-by Type=DIMENSION,Key=SERVICE` via run_bash_command. Compare yesterday's per service spend to the 7 day trailing average per service. For any service where yesterday is 2x or more the trailing average AND the dollar delta is at least 5 USD, send an SMS via send_sms_to_user listing the service, yesterday amount, and trailing average. Send nothing if no anomalies.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning SMS only when a single AWS service spiked to at least double its 7 day average yesterday with a real dollar delta.
automations.customization: Change the multiplier threshold, the minimum dollar delta, or which services to ignore.

automations.name: Substack Subscriber Cohort Note
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Writing/substack-subscribers.csv exported from Substack (columns: email, signup_date, source, post_first_seen). Build cohorts by week of signup for the past 8 weeks. For each cohort report: size, top 3 sources, top 3 entry posts. Save a refreshed snapshot to /home/workspace/Writing/cohort-snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Send a short email via send_email_to_user titled 'Substack cohorts last 8 weeks' with the table and one observation about which post or source is pulling.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday email with the past eight weeks of Substack signups broken down by cohort, top sources, and entry posts.
automations.customization: Change the cohort window, the CSV path, or how many top sources and entry posts to surface.

automations.name: Open Source Star Velocity Watch
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Projects/watched-repos.txt (lines of owner/repo). For each, run `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo} --jq '.stargazers_count'` via run_bash_command. Append today's count to /home/workspace/Projects/star-history.csv. Compute weekly delta and 4 week trailing average. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Star velocity Monday' with a table: repo, current stars, last week delta, 4 week avg delta, and a flag for any repo where this week's delta is 2x the 4 week average (heating up).
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email tracking weekly star gain across watched repos and flagging the ones suddenly heating up.
automations.customization: Change the watched repo list, the heat threshold, or the comparison window.

automations.name: The Weekly Content Atom Counter
automations.prompt: Walk /home/workspace/Writing/ and count this week's outputs by type: posted essays (files with frontmatter status: published), drafts (status: draft), captured ideas (files in /Writing/ideas/), and atomized snippets (files in /Writing/snippets/). Compare to last week's counts saved in /home/workspace/Writing/atom-counts.csv and append this week. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Content atom count' with the table and a one paragraph honest read on whether the funnel from idea to published is healthy or clogged.
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday evening email counting this week's writing pipeline by stage with an honest read on where it is clogged.
automations.customization: Change the directories scanned, the stage definitions, or the comparison window.

automations.name: Dentist Six-Month Tap
automations.prompt: Send the user an SMS that reads: 'Six-month dental tap: book your cleaning this week. Reply DONE when scheduled.' Use send_sms_to_user with no contact_name so it routes to the account owner. Keep the message under 160 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: A short SMS twice a year prompting the user to book a dental cleaning.
automations.customization: Change the months in the cron to match your last visit, or swap to Email if you ignore texts.

automations.name: Library Book Due Glance
automations.prompt: Search Gmail for messages from the last 21 days matching 'library' OR 'hold' OR 'due' OR 'overdue'. Use use_app_gmail with the gmail-search-emails tool. Pull subject, sender, and snippet for each hit. Build a single email with two sections: 'Due in the next 7 days' and 'Holds ready for pickup'. If nothing matches, send an email body that says 'No library activity this week.' Send via send_email_to_user with subject 'Library: this week'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday evening email listing upcoming due dates and ready holds from your library account emails.
automations.customization: Edit the search terms to match your specific library system's wording, or change to daily.

automations.name: Trash Night Reminder
automations.prompt: Send an SMS that reads exactly: 'Trash night. Bins to the curb before bed.' Use send_sms_to_user with no contact_name. Do not add commentary or weather context. Keep it one line.
automations.expectedOutput: A two-line SMS on collection eves so the bins go out before you forget.
automations.customization: Change the cron days to your local pickup schedule, or add recycling on alternate weeks.

automations.name: Family Birthday Two-Week Warning
automations.prompt: Use use_app_google_calendar with the google-calendar-list-events tool to read events from a calendar named 'Birthdays' or 'Family' for the window starting 14 days from today and ending 15 days from today. If exactly one birthday falls in that window, send an email titled 'Birthday in 14 days: <name>' with three suggested gift ideas under $50 and a draft text message the user could send the day-of. If no birthday falls in the window, do not send anything. Use send_email_to_user.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning email exactly 14 days before each family birthday with gift ideas and a draft message.
automations.customization: Rename the source calendar, change the lead time, or raise the gift budget.

automations.name: Friday Restaurant Pick
automations.prompt: Use web_search to find one well-reviewed restaurant within 15 minutes of the user's stored location that they have not been sent in the last 8 weeks (keep a running list at /home/workspace/Notes/restaurant-history.md, read it before picking and append the new pick after). The query should target Google Maps style review aggregators. Pick a place with 4.4+ stars and at least 50 reviews. Send an SMS with the name, the cuisine, and the approximate wait time if available.
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday afternoon SMS with one restaurant pick that has not been suggested recently.
automations.customization: Edit the rating floor, the travel radius, or the rotation window.

automations.name: Sunday Meal Prep List
automations.prompt: Read the file at /home/workspace/Home/meal-rotation.md which contains a markdown table of 14 recurring meals with their ingredient lists. Pick 4 dinners and 2 lunches that have not appeared in the last 3 weeks (track at /home/workspace/Home/meal-history.md, read before selecting and append after). Build a consolidated shopping list deduplicated by ingredient with quantities summed. Email it with subject 'Sunday meal prep, week of <date>'. Include a prep order: what to roast first, what to chop, what to portion.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday morning email with the week's meal picks and a deduplicated grocery list in prep order.
automations.customization: Edit the source rotation file or change the rotation cooldown window.

automations.name: Pollen Count Morning Alert
automations.prompt: Use web_search for 'pollen count today <user city>' from pollen.com or weather.com. Parse the tree, grass, and weed levels. If any of the three is at 'High' or 'Very High', send an SMS that names which pollen type is high and one practical step (shut windows, take antihistamine before going out, shower before bed). If all are Low or Medium, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning SMS only on days when pollen is high enough to actually matter.
automations.customization: Change the threshold to also alert on Medium, or extend to year-round.

automations.name: Oil Change Mileage Watch
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/vehicle-log.md which contains the user's last odometer reading, date, and miles-per-month estimate. Project today's estimated mileage. If the estimated mileage is within 500 of the next 5,000-mile multiple from the last oil change reading, send an email titled 'Oil change due soon' with the estimated current mileage, the target mileage, and a one-line reminder to update the log file after the next fill-up.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email only when projected mileage is near the next oil change interval.
automations.customization: Change the interval to 7,500 or 10,000 miles for synthetic oil.

automations.name: Tax Document Round-Up
automations.prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search the inbox for messages from the last 45 days matching '1099' OR 'W-2' OR 'tax document' OR 'tax form' OR '1098'. For each hit, pull sender, subject, and any attachment filenames. Group by sender. Build an email body listing what has arrived and a separate section listing common tax document types that are NOT yet present in the inbox (1099-NEC, 1099-INT, 1099-DIV, W-2, 1098 mortgage interest, brokerage 1099-B). Send via send_email_to_user with subject 'Tax docs status: <date>'.
automations.expectedOutput: Two annual emails laying out which tax forms have arrived and which are still outstanding.
automations.customization: Add or remove document types in the search list to match your specific situation.

automations.name: Vet Annual Shot Reminder
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Pets/vet-log.md which lists each pet, their last rabies date, last DHPP or FVRCP date, and last heartworm test date. If any vaccine or test is within 60 days of expiring (1 year from last date), send an email titled '<Pet name>: vet visit due in <N> days' with the specific vaccines or tests that need refreshing. If nothing is due, do not send.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email only when a pet's vaccinations or annual tests are coming due.
automations.customization: Add tests like dental cleaning or change the lead time from 60 days.

automations.name: Plant Watering Wednesday
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/plants.md which lists each plant, its watering interval in days, and its last watered date. Compute which plants need water today (last watered + interval <= today). Send an SMS listing only the plants that need water, comma-separated, with a count at the end. Example: 'Water: pothos, snake plant, monstera (3 plants).' If nothing needs water, send 'Plants happy. Nothing due.'
automations.expectedOutput: A Wednesday SMS naming exactly which plants need water this week.
automations.customization: Edit the plant list and intervals, or add a separate fertilizing schedule.

automations.name: Phone Storage Cleanup Nudge
automations.prompt: Send a Telegram message that suggests three specific cleanup actions for the user's phone storage. Rotate through this list across months: month 1 ('Delete screenshots older than 30 days, clear WhatsApp media cache, review Photos shared albums'); month 2 ('Offload unused apps, clear Safari history, delete podcast downloads you have already heard'); month 3 ('Review Downloads folder, clear voicemails, archive old iMessage threads'). Track which month was last sent at /home/workspace/Home/phone-cleanup-state.md.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly Telegram message with three rotating, specific phone cleanup actions.
automations.customization: Edit the rotation list to match Android or your own cleanup habits.

automations.name: Roth IRA Contribution Window
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Finance/retirement-log.md which lists Roth contributions for the current tax year. Compute remaining contribution room against the standard limit ($7,000, or $8,000 if user is 50+, defined in the same file). Send an email with the current contribution total, remaining room, days until the April 15 deadline for the prior tax year (only relevant in Jan-April), and the dollar amount per remaining month that would max it out.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email during contribution season with exact remaining room and a per-month pace number.
automations.customization: Change the limit, swap to Traditional IRA tracking, or add 401(k) tracking.

automations.name: Doctor Annual Physical Nudge
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Health/physicals.md which tracks the date of the last annual physical and any bloodwork. If the last physical is more than 11 months ago, send an email with the exact date of the last visit, the months elapsed, and a short script the user can paste into the patient portal to request a visit. If the most recent visit is under 11 months, do not send.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email that fires only once a year, after 11 months without a physical.
automations.customization: Change the cadence to 6 months for chronic conditions, or add specialist visits.

automations.name: Vacation Auto-Reply Setter
automations.prompt: Use use_app_google_calendar to list today's events with the keyword 'PTO', 'OOO', 'Vacation', or 'Travel' in the title. If found, use use_app_gmail with gmail-create-draft to draft a vacation auto-reply email addressed to a placeholder 'self@reminder' with subject 'OOO draft: paste into Gmail vacation responder'. Body should include the date range pulled from the event and a short message: 'Out of office until <end date>. For urgent items, contact <emergency contact placeholder>. I will reply when I return.' Do not actually enable the vacation responder; just stage the draft so the user can copy it in.
automations.expectedOutput: A Gmail draft staged any morning a PTO event is on the calendar.
automations.customization: Edit the message template or wire it to actually toggle Gmail's vacation responder via API.

automations.name: Driver License Expiry Watcher
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/ids.md which lists the expiration dates for driver license, passport, vehicle registration, and any professional licenses. For each, compute days until expiry. If anything expires within 120 days, send an email titled 'IDs expiring soon' with each item, the date, and the renewal channel (DMV online, USPS passport, county clerk, etc). If nothing is within 120 days, do not send.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email only when an ID is within four months of expiring.
automations.customization: Add fishing licenses, CDL endorsements, or change the lead time.

automations.name: Smoke Alarm Battery Twice-Yearly
automations.prompt: Send an SMS that reads: 'Smoke alarm battery swap day. Test every detector and replace any 9V older than 6 months. Reply DONE when finished.' Send via send_sms_to_user, no contact_name.
automations.expectedOutput: A twice-yearly SMS that lines up with the daylight saving change-out tradition.
automations.customization: Move the dates, add CO detector callouts, or include a checklist link.

automations.name: Refrigerator Leftover Audit
automations.prompt: Send an SMS that reads: 'Fridge audit: open the door, toss anything older than Sunday, plan a leftover dinner before grocery day.' One line. No commentary.
automations.expectedOutput: A Thursday evening SMS prompting a fridge sweep before the weekend shop.
automations.customization: Move the day to whichever night precedes your grocery run.

automations.name: Mortgage Payment Heads-Up
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Finance/recurring-bills.md which lists the mortgage due date, amount, payee, and account last 4. Send an email titled 'Mortgage due <date>: $<amount>' with a short body confirming the autopay account if listed, or instructions to schedule manually. Also list any other large bills due in the next 7 days from the same file.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email five to seven days before the mortgage hits, with adjacent large bills.
automations.customization: Change the bills tracked, the lead time, or swap to SMS.

automations.name: Streaming Subscription Audit
automations.schedule: 0 10 1 1,4,7,10 * (10am on the 1st of Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct)
automations.prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search receipts from the last 90 days from these senders: Netflix, Hulu, Disney, Max, Peacock, Paramount, Apple, Spotify, YouTube, Amazon Prime, Audible, Patreon. Build a table of service, monthly cost, and last charge date. Total them. Send an email titled 'Streaming audit: $<total>/month across <N> services' with the table sorted by cost descending and a final line suggesting the single service watched least often based on the user's notes file at /home/workspace/Home/streaming-watch.md if it exists.
automations.expectedOutput: A quarterly email listing every active streaming subscription with its real cost.
automations.customization: Add or remove sender names, or change to monthly.

automations.name: Free Trial Cancel Day Alarm
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Finance/free-trials.md which is a markdown table with columns: Service, Trial Start Date, Trial Length Days, Cancel URL. Compute days remaining for each (trial start + length - today). If any trial has exactly 2 days remaining, send an SMS naming the service and the cancel URL. If nothing matches, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: An SMS exactly two days before any tracked free trial converts to a paid subscription.
automations.customization: Change the lead time to 1 or 3 days, or switch to Email.

automations.name: Daylight Saving Clock Sweep
automations.schedule: 0 11 8 3 0 (11am on the 2nd Sunday of March, approximate)
automations.prompt: Send an email titled 'Clock sweep checklist' with a list of every clock and timed device in a typical home that does not auto-adjust: oven, microwave, car dashboards, wall clocks, wristwatches, smoke alarm test buttons, sprinkler timers, thermostats without wifi, coffee maker. Add a final line: 'Test smoke alarms while you are at it.'
automations.expectedOutput: An email twice a year listing every analog clock and timer that needs human adjustment.
automations.customization: Edit the device list, add the November date, or convert to SMS.

automations.name: Lawn Mowing Weather Window
automations.prompt: Use web_search to pull a 4-day forecast for the user's city. Identify the day in the next 4 days with the lowest rain probability and a high temperature between 55 and 85 F. Send an SMS naming that day, the rain percentage, and the high. Example: 'Best mow window: Saturday, 10% rain, 72F.'
automations.expectedOutput: A Thursday SMS picking the best lawn-mowing day from the upcoming weekend window.
automations.customization: Adjust the temperature range, the day-of-week trigger, or the forecast horizon.

automations.name: Family Group Catch-Up Nudge
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Personal/family-touch-log.md which lists each family member and the last date you contacted them. Pick the one person who has gone the longest without contact and is over 14 days. Send an SMS that names them and suggests one concrete opening line (a question about their recent thing, a memory, a photo to send). After sending, append today's date to that person's row.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday SMS naming one family member who is overdue for contact, with an opener.
automations.customization: Edit the family list, change the threshold from 14 days, or switch to weekly digest.

automations.name: Anniversary Surprise Lead Time
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Personal/anniversaries.md which lists each anniversary (wedding, dating, first date, etc) with month-day. If any anniversary is exactly 21 days out, send an email with the anniversary name, the date, and three planning prompts: 'What did you do last year, what would surprise them, what would they actually want.' Include one reservation reminder and one gift idea bucket. If no anniversary is 21 days out, do not send.
automations.expectedOutput: An email exactly 21 days before each anniversary with planning prompts.
automations.customization: Change the lead time, add more anniversaries, or add a 7-day follow-up.

automations.name: Weekly Town Council Agenda Pull
automations.prompt: Use web_search to find the user's town or city council agenda for the upcoming week. The query should be '<user city> town council agenda <current month> <current year>'. Use read_webpage on the most authoritative result (city .gov domain preferred). Extract: meeting date, time, location, agenda item titles. Send an email with a clean bulleted list of agenda items and any item that mentions zoning, taxes, budget, or police, flagged in bold.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email summarizing the upcoming town council meeting agenda with flagged hot topics.
automations.customization: Change the city, the flagged keywords, or the day of the week.

automations.name: Local Election Two-Week Warning
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Civic/elections.md which lists upcoming election dates for the user's state and county. If any election is exactly 14 days out, use web_search to find the official sample ballot for that election in the user's county (search '<county> <state> sample ballot <election date>'). Send an email titled 'Vote in 14 days: <election name>' with the polling location lookup link for the user's state and a bulleted list of ballot items pulled from the sample ballot. If no election matches, do not send.
automations.expectedOutput: An email exactly two weeks out from any tracked local or federal election, with the ballot preview.
automations.customization: Edit the elections file, change the lead time, or add early voting deadlines.

automations.name: Stripe MRR Daily Snapshot
automations.prompt: Run a bash command that calls the Stripe API (curl with the STRIPE_SECRET_KEY env var) to fetch active subscriptions (/v1/subscriptions?status=active&limit=100, paginate). Sum the monthly value across all active subs, normalizing yearly to monthly by dividing by 12. Save today's MRR to /home/workspace/Finance/mrr-history.csv as a new row (date, mrr_usd, active_subs). Compute delta vs yesterday and delta vs 30 days ago from the CSV. Send an email titled 'MRR: $<value> (<+/->$<delta> day, <+/->$<delta> 30d)' with a 7-day sparkline drawn in ASCII characters.
automations.expectedOutput: A daily email with current MRR, day-over-day delta, 30-day delta, and a 7-day ASCII trend line.
automations.customization: Add ARR, customer count, churn count, or split by product.

automations.name: Vercel Build Failure Watch
automations.prompt: Run a bash command that calls the Vercel API (curl with VERCEL_TOKEN env var) at /v6/deployments?limit=10. Parse the JSON and check the readyState of the most recent deployment per project. If any project's most recent deployment has state ERROR or CANCELED, and you have not already alerted on this deployment id (track at /home/workspace/Ops/vercel-alert-state.json), send an SMS naming the project and the deployment URL. Save the deployment id after alerting so you do not double-fire.
automations.expectedOutput: An SMS the moment any tracked Vercel project's latest deploy fails, fired once per deployment.
automations.customization: Filter to specific project names, change the polling interval, or route to Discord.

automations.name: Cloudflare DNS Record Drift
automations.prompt: Run a bash script that calls the Cloudflare API (curl with CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN env var) listing DNS records for every zone the token can read. Snapshot the full record set to /home/workspace/Ops/dns-snapshot.json. Diff against the previous snapshot at /home/workspace/Ops/dns-snapshot.prev.json. If any record was added, removed, or changed (type, content, proxied), send an email titled 'DNS drift detected in <zone>' with the diff in a readable format. Rotate the current snapshot to .prev after the diff.
automations.expectedOutput: An email only when DNS records change across any zone you manage.
automations.customization: Add an allowlist of records expected to change, or scope to one zone.

automations.name: NPM Audit Vulnerability Sweep
automations.prompt: For each repo path listed in /home/workspace/Dev/repos.txt, run 'npm audit --json' (or 'pnpm audit --json' if pnpm-lock.yaml present) and capture output. Parse total vulnerability counts by severity. Build a report at /home/workspace/Dev/audit-reports/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md listing each repo with critical/high/moderate/low counts and the top 5 advisories with their fix availability. Email a summary listing only repos with any critical or high vulnerabilities, ordered by critical count desc.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email and dated markdown file showing every repo with critical or high npm vulnerabilities.
automations.customization: Add pip-audit for Python repos, raise the severity threshold, or auto-open PRs for fixes.

automations.name: SaaS Trial-to-Paid Drop-Off Watch
automations.prompt: Query the user's Postgres or SQLite app database via psql or sqlite3 (connection string in DATABASE_URL env var) for users whose trial ended in the last 24 hours. For each, fetch their signup date, their last login, the number of core actions taken during trial (defined in /home/workspace/Dev/trial-events.sql), and whether they converted to paid. Build a table grouped by 'Converted' and 'Did Not Convert', sorted by core-action count desc within each group. Email with subject 'Trial cohort: <date>, <X> converted of <Y>'.
automations.expectedOutput: A daily email showing yesterday's expiring trial cohort, who converted, and how active each was.
automations.customization: Change the conversion windows, the event definitions, or add per-source attribution.

automations.name: ProductHunt Top 5 Recap
automations.prompt: Use read_webpage on https://www.producthunt.com/ to grab today's top 5 products by upvote count. For each, extract name, tagline, upvote count, and category tag. Send a Telegram message with each product on its own block, formatted like: '1. <name> (<votes> upvotes) - <tagline>'. Include the ProductHunt URL of the page at the bottom.
automations.expectedOutput: An evening Telegram digest of today's top 5 ProductHunt launches.
automations.customization: Change to top 10, filter by category tag, or switch to Discord.

automations.name: Backlog Aging Heat Map
automations.prompt: Use use_app_linear to list all open issues in the user's primary team across all projects. For each issue, compute days since createdAt. Bucket into: 0-7 days, 8-21 days, 22-60 days, 61-180 days, 180+ days. Build an HTML email with one bar per bucket, the bar width proportional to issue count, and a list of the 5 oldest issues with title, days old, and assignee. Subject: 'Backlog aging: <N> issues, oldest <days>d'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday morning email with a visual age distribution of your Linear backlog plus the 5 oldest items.
automations.customization: Filter by project, label, or priority, and change the age buckets.

automations.name: Cold Outreach Reply Detector
automations.prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search for replies in the last 2 hours to a specific outreach thread label, configured at /home/workspace/Sales/outreach-label.txt (default: 'Outreach/Sent'). For each new reply, classify the response into one of: Positive (interested, asks a question), Neutral (acknowledgment), Negative (not interested, unsubscribe), Auto-reply. Send an SMS only for Positive replies, with the sender name, company if extractable from the signature, and the first 80 characters of the reply.
automations.expectedOutput: An SMS within 2 hours of any positive reply landing on a tracked outreach thread, weekdays only.
automations.customization: Change the label, the polling cadence, or add Negative replies for damage control.

automations.name: Postgres Slow Query Hunter
automations.prompt: Run psql (DATABASE_URL env var) to query pg_stat_statements for the top 10 queries by total_exec_time over the last 7 days where calls > 50. For each, capture: normalized query, total time, mean time, calls, rows. Build an email with the table sorted by total_exec_time desc, plus a short note flagging any query with mean_exec_time > 500ms. Reset stats only if explicitly told via /home/workspace/Ops/reset-stats.flag existing.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email naming the 10 most expensive Postgres queries from the past week.
automations.customization: Change the window, add per-database breakdowns, or route to Discord.

automations.name: Discord Community Pulse
automations.prompt: For each channel id listed in /home/workspace/Community/discord-channels.json, use get_discord_history to pull the last 7 days of messages (limit 1000). Compute: total message count, unique active members, top 5 emojis used, top 5 most-replied-to messages. Build an email with one section per channel showing those numbers. At the bottom, list any new members whose first message appeared in the last 7 days. Subject: 'Discord pulse: <total> messages across <N> channels'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email summarizing last week's Discord activity per channel with new-member callouts.
automations.customization: Change the channels, the window, or add sentiment analysis of top threads.

automations.name: Founder Inbox VIP Tier Sort
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Inbox/vip-tiers.json which has three tiers: Tier 1 (investors, top customers), Tier 2 (warm leads, advisors), Tier 3 (everyone else). Use use_app_gmail to list unread emails from the last 24 hours. Match each sender to a tier. Build an email with three clearly separated sections, Tier 1 first, listing sender, subject, and one-line auto-generated reason it likely matters. Drop Tier 3 entirely unless it is empty above. Subject: 'Inbox: <T1> Tier 1, <T2> Tier 2, <T3> Tier 3'.
automations.expectedOutput: A twice-daily email organizing unread mail by your hand-built VIP tiers, with Tier 3 hidden.
automations.customization: Edit the tier list, change the polling cadence, or add a Tier 0 for spouse and family.

automations.name: Weekly Competitor Pricing Page Diff
automations.prompt: Read the list of competitor pricing page URLs at /home/workspace/Competitors/pricing-urls.txt (one URL per line). For each URL, use read_webpage to fetch the page as markdown. Save the new content to /home/workspace/Competitors/snapshots/<domain>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md. Diff against the most recent prior snapshot for that domain. If any pricing-relevant string changed (any line containing '$', '/mo', '/year', 'free', or 'plan'), include the before/after lines in the email body. Group by domain. If no diffs anywhere, send an email body 'No pricing changes detected this week.'
automations.expectedOutput: A Tuesday email showing every competitor pricing page change since last week, with diffs.
automations.customization: Add or remove competitor URLs, change the diff keywords, or move to daily.

automations.name: GitHub Star Velocity Tracker
automations.prompt: For each repo listed in /home/workspace/Dev/watched-repos.txt (format: owner/repo, one per line), run 'gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>' and capture stargazers_count. Append today's count to /home/workspace/Dev/star-history.csv (repo, date, stars). Compute 1-day, 7-day, and 30-day star deltas from the CSV. Build an email sorted by 7-day delta desc, table format. Flag any repo with a 1-day delta > 3 standard deviations above its 30-day mean as 'spiking'.
automations.expectedOutput: A daily email ranking watched repos by 7-day star velocity with spike callouts.
automations.customization: Add forks and watchers, change the spike threshold, or scope to your own repos.

automations.name: Reddit Brand Mention Sweep
automations.prompt: Read the list of brand terms at /home/workspace/Brand/mention-terms.txt (product name, founder handle, domain, common misspellings). For each term, use web_search with query 'site:reddit.com "<term>"' restricted to the last 4 hours via the time_range parameter. For each new hit not already in /home/workspace/Brand/seen-urls.txt, send a Telegram message with the subreddit, the post title, the URL, and the first 200 chars. Append the URL to the seen file after sending.
automations.expectedOutput: A Telegram ping within 4 hours of any new Reddit mention of your tracked brand terms.
automations.customization: Add Hacker News and X mentions, change the cadence, or filter by subreddit.

automations.name: Self-Improving Prompt Library Audit
automations.prompt: List every file in /home/workspace/Prompts/ recursively. For each .md prompt file, read it and check three things: does it have a 'last_used' date in frontmatter, has it been used in the last 60 days (cross-reference /home/workspace/Prompts/usage-log.csv), and does the prompt contain any token from /home/workspace/Prompts/forbidden-tokens.txt (one word per line, user-maintained slop blocklist). Build a report at /home/workspace/Prompts/audit-<YYYY-MM>.md listing: prompts unused 60+ days, prompts that hit the blocklist, prompts missing frontmatter. Email a summary count of each bucket with the file paths.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email and dated audit file flagging stale, sloppy, or under-documented prompts.
automations.customization: Edit the forbidden-tokens file, change the staleness window, or add token-length flags.

automations.name: Launch Week Engagement Decay Chart
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Marketing/active-launches.json which lists launch posts with: post_id, post_url, launch_date, end_date. Only run if today is within any active launch window. For each active launch post, use x_search to fetch current impressions, likes, reposts, replies. Append today's snapshot to /home/workspace/Marketing/launch-metrics.csv. Build an email with one section per launch showing day-by-day numbers and an ASCII chart of impressions per day. Flag the first day the daily delta drops below 25% of peak as 'decay onset'.
automations.expectedOutput: A daily email during any active launch window tracking the decay curve of each launch post.
automations.customization: Add cross-posts on LinkedIn and Threads, or change the decay threshold.

automations.name: Hacker News Show Pull
automations.prompt: Use read_webpage on https://news.ycombinator.com/show to fetch today's Show HN front page. Extract the top 10 by points: title, points, comment count, and submitter. For each, also fetch the submission URL (the link out of HN, not the comments page). Build an email titled 'Show HN today: top 10' with each item as a block (title as link to comments, points, comment count, one-line snippet from the linked site if accessible). Skip items with fewer than 10 points.
automations.expectedOutput: An evening email with the top 10 Show HN projects of the day, scored and linked.
automations.customization: Change to Ask HN or the main front page, raise the points floor, or move to Telegram.

automations.name: The 5pm Dinner Decider
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/pantry.md and /home/workspace/Home/dietary-notes.md. Pick three dinner options that use mostly what is already in the pantry and fit the dietary notes. For each option, list the missing ingredients (max two per dish) and a rough cook time. If everything in the pantry is depleted, run a web_search for a single 20 minute dinner recipe with five or fewer ingredients. Send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user with the three options as a short numbered list, no preamble.
automations.expectedOutput: A short text at 5pm with three dinner picks based on what is actually in the pantry, plus a short shopping nudge if needed.
automations.customization: Edit pantry.md and dietary-notes.md, or change the number of options and cook time threshold in the prompt.

automations.name: Saturday Morning Cleanup Captain
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/cleanup-rotation.md, which contains a list of weekly chores grouped by room. Pick the next three rooms in rotation that have not been touched in the longest time according to the last_done dates. Build a short Saturday checklist: each chore as a one line action with an estimated minute count. Update cleanup-rotation.md with today as last_done for the chosen rooms. Email the checklist via send_email_to_user with subject 'Saturday cleanup, 3 rooms' and the list as a markdown checklist.
automations.expectedOutput: A Saturday morning email with three rooms to clean and a short timed checklist that rotates fairly through the house.
automations.customization: Edit cleanup-rotation.md to add or remove rooms and chores, or change how many rooms get picked each week.

automations.name: Birthday Card Mail-By Reminder
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/birthdays.md, which lists friends and family with name, birthday, and shipping zone. For each entry, compute days until the next birthday. If exactly 10 days remain, that person needs a card sent by today to arrive in time. Build one email listing only those people, each with name, date, and a one line gift idea drawn from the notes column in the file. If nobody is at 10 days, send no email. Use send_email_to_user with subject 'Birthday cards to mail this week' only when there is at least one match.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning email only on days when someone needs a card mailed today to arrive on time.
automations.customization: Change the 10 day lead window, swap shipping zones, or add a notes column with gift ideas in birthdays.md.

automations.name: Where Did I Put That Log
automations.prompt: Open /home/workspace/Home/where-is-it.md. This file is appended to throughout the day via voice notes or quick edits with lines like 'passport: top drawer of camper desk'. Deduplicate entries, keeping only the most recent location for each item. Sort alphabetically by item name. Write the cleaned version back to the same file with a header showing today's date as 'Last cleaned: YYYY-MM-DD'. Do not send any message.
automations.expectedOutput: A clean, deduped index of where household items live, updated quietly every night.
automations.customization: Add categories like 'tools' or 'documents' as sections in the file, or change the cleanup time.

automations.name: The Did I Text Mom Check
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/keep-in-touch.md. Each row lists a person, their preferred channel, and last_contacted date. Find anyone marked 'weekly cadence' who has not been contacted in 7 or more days. If the list is empty, send a one line SMS: 'All weekly people are current.' Otherwise send an SMS via send_sms_to_user listing each overdue person on its own line with their preferred channel and how many days it has been. Keep the whole SMS under 320 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday evening text showing exactly which weekly-cadence people are overdue for a check-in.
automations.customization: Add or remove cadence tiers like 'monthly' or 'quarterly' and adjust the day threshold in the prompt.

automations.name: Weekend Hobby Spark
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/hobbies.md, which lists hobbies the user actually enjoys with rough skill level. Pick one at random, weighted toward hobbies not touched in the last month based on last_done dates. Run a web_search for one beginner-friendly project or activity for that hobby that can be done in 2 to 3 hours. Email a short note with the suggested project, the rough time, and any materials needed. Use send_email_to_user with subject 'Saturday hobby spark: <hobby name>'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Saturday morning email with one concrete weekend project drawn from your real hobby list.
automations.customization: Edit hobbies.md with skill levels and last_done dates, or change the time-budget bounds for suggestions.

automations.name: Mood and Weather Pattern Log
automations.prompt: Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user asking: 'Mood today 1 to 5? Reply with a number and one word.' When the user replies, web_search today's local weather summary for Salmon, Idaho (high temp, low temp, conditions, barometric pressure). Append a new row to /home/workspace/Health/mood-weather.csv with columns date, mood, word, high, low, conditions, pressure. If the file does not exist, create it with that header row.
automations.expectedOutput: A nightly check-in text that quietly builds a long-term CSV of mood against local weather.
automations.customization: Change the mood scale, ask different free-text questions, or switch the location used for the weather lookup.

automations.name: Friday Fridge Cleanout
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/fridge.md, where each line is an item with a purchase or open date. Find items older than 5 days for fresh produce, 7 days for opened sauces, and any explicitly tagged 'leftover' older than 3 days. Build a short SMS listing the top five items most at risk of going to waste, ordered by age, and one suggested combo dinner that uses two of them. Send via send_sms_to_user. If nothing is at risk, send 'Fridge is fine.'
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday afternoon text with the five oldest fridge items and a quick combo idea before the weekend.
automations.customization: Edit the age thresholds per category and add new tags like 'meal-prep' or 'dairy' in fridge.md.

automations.name: The Anti-Affirmation Scrambler
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Personal/voice-lines.md, which contains 30 to 50 short lines written by the user in their own voice (dry, honest, occasionally funny, not motivational). Pick one at random that has not been used in the last 14 days according to /home/workspace/Personal/voice-lines-used.csv. Append today's date and the line to that CSV. Send the line as a single SMS via send_sms_to_user with no quotes and no preamble.
automations.expectedOutput: A 7am text with one honest one-liner from your own writing, rotated so nothing repeats inside two weeks.
automations.customization: Edit voice-lines.md to add or remove lines, or change the no-repeat window from 14 days.

automations.name: Doctor Visit Prep Sheet
automations.prompt: Use use_app_google_calendar to list events on tomorrow's primary calendar. If any event title contains 'doctor', 'dentist', 'clinic', 'neurologist', or 'appointment', build a one page prep sheet. Pull recent entries from /home/workspace/Health/symptom-log.md (last 14 days), current med list from /home/workspace/Health/meds.md, and a short questions section from /home/workspace/Health/questions.md. Email the sheet with subject 'Prep for tomorrow: <event title>' via send_email_to_user. If no matching event exists, do nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: An evening email the night before any medical appointment with symptoms, meds, and questions on one page.
automations.customization: Edit the trigger keywords or the files that feed the prep sheet, and adjust the symptom lookback window.

automations.name: The Lazy Sunday Movie Picker
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/watchlist.md, which has movies and shows the user has been meaning to watch with genre and length tags. Filter to anything under 110 minutes and tagged 'comfort' or 'low effort'. If the list has fewer than three items, web_search for one current well-reviewed feel-good movie under two hours. Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with three picks, one per line, including title and runtime.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday evening text with three low-effort, short-runtime movie picks already aligned with your taste.
automations.customization: Edit watchlist.md, change the runtime limit, or adjust which tags count as 'comfort' viewing.

automations.name: Library Book Due Date Watcher
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/library-books.md. Each entry has title, branch, and due_date. Compute days until due for each book. If any book is due in 2 days or less and not yet returned, send a short email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Library books due soon' listing each book, branch, and exact due date. If nothing qualifies, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A quiet morning email only when library books are within two days of being late.
automations.customization: Adjust the warning window, add a renewal link column, or split alerts by branch.

automations.name: Camper Battery Voltage Logger
automations.prompt: Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user asking: 'Camper bank voltage? Reply with a number like 12.6'. When the user replies with a number, append a row to /home/workspace/Camper/battery-log.csv with columns timestamp, voltage, hour_slot. If the file does not exist, create it with that header. If the reply is below 12.2 in winter months (Nov to Mar) or below 12.0 otherwise, send a follow-up SMS: 'Low bank, consider charging today.'
automations.expectedOutput: Two daily voltage check-ins that build a CSV log and warn when the camper bank is sagging.
automations.customization: Change the warning thresholds per season or add additional fields like solar input and amp draw.

automations.name: The Pantry Inventory Refresh
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/pantry.md and /home/workspace/Home/pantry-purchases.csv. Cross reference purchase dates with shelf life ranges in /home/workspace/Home/shelf-life.md. Flag any pantry item past its expected shelf life and any staple (rice, beans, flour, oats, oil, salt, pasta sauce) missing from pantry.md. Email a single Sunday note via send_email_to_user with two sections, 'Use or toss' and 'Restock'. Update pantry.md last_reviewed date.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday morning email pairing pantry items that should be used up with staples that need restocking.
automations.customization: Edit shelf-life.md, add or remove items from the staples list, or change which day the review runs.

automations.name: Family Group Text Opener
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/family-context.md (a short page of low-stakes safe topics and shared interests). Pick one topic from the list. Optionally run a web_search for one current, friendly, non-political news item on that topic. Draft a single warm one or two sentence opener the user could paste into the family group text. Send it via send_sms_to_user as just the draft, no labels.
automations.expectedOutput: A Wednesday evening text with one ready-to-paste opener for the family group chat.
automations.customization: Edit family-context.md to control safe topics, or change the schedule to a different evening.

automations.name: Dog Walk Route Randomizer
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Pets/walk-routes.md, which lists named walking routes near Salmon with rough length, terrain, and shade notes. Run a web_search for today's local weather in Salmon, Idaho. If high temp is above 80F, pick a shaded short route; if below 30F or precipitation is likely, pick a short low-elevation route; otherwise pick something the dog has not done in the last 5 days. Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the route name, expected length, and a one line reason.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning text picking the right named walking route for the weather and rotation.
automations.customization: Edit walk-routes.md to add routes or tune the temperature thresholds and rotation window.

automations.name: Monthly Phone Photo Backup Nudge
automations.prompt: Use use_app_google_drive to list files in the 'Phone Backups' folder. Find the most recent upload date. If the newest upload is older than 35 days, send an email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Phone photos not backed up in over a month' explaining the gap and including a checklist of three concrete steps (plug in phone, open backup app, verify folder size). If the last backup is fresh, send a short 'Photo backups look current' confirmation.
automations.expectedOutput: A monthly email that either nudges you to back up phone photos or confirms it is up to date.
automations.customization: Change the Drive folder name, the gap threshold in days, or the checklist contents.

automations.name: The Lazy What I Ate Log
automations.prompt: Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user asking 'What did you eat in the last few hours? One line, lazy is fine.' Wait for a reply. Append the timestamp and reply text to /home/workspace/Health/food-log.csv with columns timestamp, slot, text. The 1pm prompt uses slot=midday and the 8pm prompt uses slot=evening. Do not analyze, do not score, do not lecture.
automations.expectedOutput: Two short daily texts that build a no-judgment running log of what you actually ate.
automations.customization: Change the wording, the number of pings per day, or the slot labels in the CSV.

automations.name: Plant Watering Days Counter
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/plants.md, which lists each plant with location and watering interval (days). Read /home/workspace/Home/plant-log.csv to find last_watered per plant. For any plant where today is greater than last_watered + interval, build a single SMS line per plant in the form 'snake plant (camper window) is due'. Send one combined SMS via send_sms_to_user only if at least one plant is due. After sending, do not auto-update last_watered; the user does that manually when they actually water.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning text only on days when at least one plant is actually due to be watered.
automations.customization: Edit plants.md to add plants and intervals, or split alerts by indoor vs outdoor.

automations.name: Friday Wind-Down Playlist
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Personal/music-taste.md, which lists genres the user actually likes and a few anchor artists. Build a 12 track wind-down playlist concept that fits a 'slow Friday evening' mood. Use web_search if helpful to confirm artist names and recent albums, but do not invent tracks; if uncertain, prefer well known catalog songs. Email the list via send_email_to_user with subject 'Friday wind-down, 12 tracks' as a markdown numbered list of 'Artist - Title'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday evening email with a 12 track wind-down playlist that fits your actual taste.
automations.customization: Edit music-taste.md, change the track count, or shift the mood (focus, drive, deep work).

automations.name: Tax Receipt Photo Catcher
automations.prompt: Use use_app_google_drive to list files in the 'Receipts inbox' folder added since the last run (track last_run in /home/workspace/Finance/receipts-state.json). For each new file, extract the filename and upload date. Append rows to /home/workspace/Finance/receipts.csv with columns date_added, filename, drive_link, category (blank). Update last_run. Email a short Sunday summary via send_email_to_user with the count of new receipts and the first five filenames for spot checking.
automations.expectedOutput: A weekly email confirming how many receipts were filed and prompting you to tag categories.
automations.customization: Change the Drive folder, add automatic category guessing, or switch to a monthly cadence.

automations.name: Car Maintenance Mileage Nudge
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/vehicle.md, which has current odometer (manually updated), last_oil_change_miles, oil_interval, last_tire_rotation_miles, rotation_interval, and registration_due date. If current odometer minus last_oil_change_miles exceeds oil_interval minus 300, flag oil change. Same for tire rotation. If registration is due within 30 days, flag registration. If any flags, send email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Camper truck maintenance due soon' listing flagged items. Otherwise send a one line SMS via send_sms_to_user: 'Truck is current.'
automations.expectedOutput: A weekly check that emails when service is approaching and texts a clean bill when it is not.
automations.customization: Update vehicle.md with new intervals, add brake pad mileage, or change the lead-time buffers.

automations.name: Friend Birthday Card Drafter
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/friends.md, which lists name, birthday, vibe (dry, sincere, goofy), and a short notes column. Find anyone whose birthday is exactly 3 days away. For each match, draft one short handwritten-card message (3 to 5 sentences) in their preferred vibe, using one specific detail from the notes column. If nobody matches, do nothing. Otherwise email all drafts together via send_email_to_user with subject 'Card drafts to write today'.
automations.expectedOutput: An email three days before a friend's birthday with a card draft tuned to that specific person.
automations.customization: Adjust the lead time, the vibe categories, or the message length range.

automations.name: Hourly Move Your Body Ping
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Health/micro-movements.md, which lists 20 micro-movements (each 30 to 90 seconds, no equipment, low impact). Pick one not used in the last 4 hours according to /home/workspace/Health/micro-movements-log.csv. Append the pick to the log. Send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user with just the movement name and the duration, no preamble. Keep tone matter of fact, not chirpy.
automations.expectedOutput: Short workday texts with one tiny movement each hour, rotating without repeats.
automations.customization: Edit micro-movements.md, change the active hours, or skip the prompt entirely on focus-block days.

automations.name: Saturday Farmers Market Forecast
automations.prompt: Run a web_search for 'Salmon Idaho farmers market today' and for the local weather forecast for Salmon, Idaho on Saturday morning. Read /home/workspace/Home/market-staples.md for the user's usual buys (eggs, greens, bread, etc) by season. Build an email with the market hours and location (if found), the weather summary, and a short Saturday shopping list pulled from market-staples.md filtered to the current season. Send via send_email_to_user with subject 'Saturday market notes'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Saturday morning email with market hours, weather, and your usual seasonal shopping list.
automations.customization: Edit market-staples.md per season, change the city, or drop the weather section if unwanted.

automations.name: Holiday Gift List Builder
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/friends.md and /home/workspace/Contacts/family-context.md for shared interests and notes. Build a holiday gift planning sheet listing each person, a one line vibe summary, a target spend tier (under 25, 25 to 75, 75 plus) drawn from a 'tier' column, and three gift ideas grounded in their notes. Write the sheet to /home/workspace/Personal/gift-list-<year>.md and email a short preview via send_email_to_user with the file path and a count of people covered.
automations.expectedOutput: A November 1 email pointing to a fully drafted holiday gift list grounded in your real contacts.
automations.customization: Adjust spend tiers, expand to coworkers or neighbors, or move the kickoff date earlier.

automations.name: Vercel Deploy Failure Sentinel
automations.prompt: Run a bash curl against the Vercel API (https://api.vercel.com/v6/deployments) using $VERCEL_TOKEN to list the latest 10 deployments across configured projects. Parse JSON for any state of ERROR, CANCELED, or BUILD_ERROR newer than the last_seen timestamp in /home/.z/state/vercel-deploys.json. For each new failure, send one concise SMS via send_sms_to_user: '<project> <branch> failed at <time UTC>, see <inspectorUrl>'. Update last_seen. Skip projects with state READY.
automations.expectedOutput: A near-realtime SMS only when a Vercel build actually fails, with the project, branch, and link.
automations.customization: Filter to specific project IDs, change the polling interval, or add Slack delivery alongside SMS.

automations.name: Supabase Row Count Drift Watch
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Projects/db-monitor/tables.yml for the list of Supabase tables to track. For each table, run a bash curl against the Supabase REST API using $SUPABASE_URL and $SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY with Prefer: count=exact and limit=0 to read the Content-Range header. Append today's row count per table to /home/workspace/Projects/db-monitor/counts.csv. Compute day-over-day delta. If any table dropped by more than 5 percent or grew by more than 200 percent, email a short report via send_email_to_user with subject 'Row count drift alert'.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning email only when a tracked Supabase table shows an unusual day-over-day row count change.
automations.customization: Tune the drift thresholds per table, add hourly counts, or pipe alerts to Discord.

automations.name: Pre-Launch Checklist Verifier
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Projects/launch/checklist.yml, which lists checks: HTTPS reachable on production URL, /robots.txt returns 200, /sitemap.xml returns 200, og:image meta tag present in HTML, Plausible script tag present, primary form POST returns 2xx. Run each check via curl in bash. Build a markdown report with pass/fail per check, then email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Pre-launch checks: <pass count>/<total>'. Save the same report to /home/workspace/Projects/launch/last-report.md.
automations.expectedOutput: A three-times-weekly email scoring your launch checklist against the live site.
automations.customization: Add or remove checks in checklist.yml, change the cadence, or fail loudly with SMS on red checks.

automations.name: Cron Last-Run Auditor
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Ops/cron-registry.yml, which lists each scheduled job by name with expected_max_gap_minutes and a heartbeat file path under /home/workspace/Ops/heartbeats/. Run bash 'stat -c %Y' on each heartbeat file to find the last modification time. Compute minutes since last run. For any job where minutes_since_last_run exceeds expected_max_gap_minutes, include it in an email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Stale cron jobs: <count>'. If nothing is stale, send nothing.
automations.expectedOutput: A daily email only when one or more cron-style jobs missed their expected window.
automations.customization: Update cron-registry.yml as you add jobs, or tighten the per-job allowable gap.

automations.name: NPM Downloads Pulse
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Projects/oss/packages.yml for a list of npm package names you maintain. For each, curl https://api.npmjs.org/downloads/range/last-week/<package> and parse daily counts. Compute last-week total and week-over-week change vs the prior week. Append a row per package to /home/workspace/Projects/oss/npm-pulse.csv. Email a Monday summary via send_email_to_user with subject 'NPM weekly pulse' showing each package, last-week downloads, and percentage change as a small markdown table.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday morning email with weekly download counts and week-over-week changes for your npm packages.
automations.customization: Add packages to the YAML, change the time window from last-week to last-month, or post to Discord.

automations.name: Pull Request Aging Report
automations.prompt: Use the gh CLI in bash to run 'gh pr list --repo <owner/repo> --state open --json number,title,author,createdAt,updatedAt,reviewDecision' for each repo listed in /home/workspace/Projects/oss/repos.txt. Compute days_open and days_since_update. Bucket PRs into 'fresh' (under 7 days), 'aging' (7 to 21 days), and 'stale' (over 21 days). Build a markdown report grouped by repo then bucket. Email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Open PRs by age, Friday review'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday afternoon email grouping your open PRs by repo and age bucket.
automations.customization: Filter to PRs needing your review, exclude draft PRs, or change the age bucket boundaries.

automations.name: Stripe MRR Movement Digest
automations.prompt: Run a bash curl against the Stripe API (https://api.stripe.com/v1/subscriptions?status=active&limit=100) using $STRIPE_SECRET_KEY. Paginate as needed. Sum recurring amount normalized to monthly to compute today's MRR. Read yesterday's MRR from /home/workspace/Finance/mrr-history.csv. Append today's row (date, mrr_cents, active_subs, new_today, canceled_today). Email a short digest via send_email_to_user with subject 'MRR <amount>, <delta>' showing the daily delta, new subscriptions, and cancellations.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning email with current MRR, daily change, new signups, and cancellations.
automations.customization: Switch to test mode keys, add ARR, or include churn rate from a 30 day window.

automations.name: Cloudflare Cache Hit Rate Watch
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Ops/cloudflare-zones.yml for zone IDs to monitor. For each zone, run a bash curl against the Cloudflare GraphQL Analytics API using $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN to fetch cache status counts over the last 6 hours. Compute hit_rate = hits / (hits + misses). Append rows to /home/workspace/Ops/cf-cache.csv. If any zone has hit_rate below 0.70 and request volume above 500, POST a JSON alert payload to $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL summarizing the zone, the rate, and the volume.
automations.expectedOutput: A Slack ping only when a Cloudflare zone's cache hit rate drops below 70 percent at meaningful volume.
automations.customization: Adjust the hit-rate floor, the volume floor, or the polling interval per zone.

automations.name: The Docs Drift Detector
automations.prompt: For each repo in /home/workspace/Projects/oss/repos.txt, run bash 'gh api repos/<repo>/commits?per_page=30' to fetch recent commits. Read /home/workspace/Projects/oss/docs-paths.yml for per-repo code globs (src/**, lib/**) and docs globs (README.md, docs/**). Using git diff between HEAD and the last documented release tag, compute lines changed in code paths vs docs paths over the last 30 days. Flag repos where code lines changed exceeds 200 and docs lines changed is under 20. Email findings via send_email_to_user with subject 'Docs drift candidates'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Tuesday email listing repos whose code has moved fast while docs have not.
automations.customization: Tune the line thresholds, change the lookback window, or add per-repo overrides.

automations.name: SEO Title Tag Auditor
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Projects/seo/pages.txt for URLs to audit. For each URL, run bash curl and parse the <title> and <meta name="description"> tags. Check: title length 40 to 60 characters, description 120 to 160, no duplicate titles across the list, no missing description. Append a row per page to /home/workspace/Projects/seo/title-audit.csv. Email a Monday report via send_email_to_user with subject 'SEO title audit, <issue count> issues' showing only the failing pages and the specific issue.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email listing only the pages whose title or description tags fail length or uniqueness checks.
automations.customization: Edit pages.txt, change length bounds, or add open graph image checks.

automations.name: Discord Standout-Messages Pulse
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Projects/community/discord-channels.yml. For each tracked channel, run a bash curl against the Discord API (GET /channels/<id>/messages?limit=100) using $DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN to fetch the last week of messages. Compute messages_count, unique_authors, top 3 most-reacted messages. Append weekly rollups to /home/workspace/Projects/community/discord-pulse.csv. Email a Monday digest via send_email_to_user with subject 'Discord weekly pulse' summarizing each channel and quoting the top reacted message per channel.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email summarizing message volume, active members, and standout messages per Discord channel.
automations.customization: Add or drop channels, change the lookback window, or surface unanswered questions.

automations.name: Founder Sleep Debt Tracker
automations.prompt: Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user asking 'Hours slept last night? Reply with a number, decimals fine.' Append the reply with date to /home/workspace/Health/sleep.csv. Compute a 7 day moving average and a running sleep debt (target_hours - actual, summed over 7 days, target_hours from /home/workspace/Health/sleep-target.txt). If sleep debt exceeds 5 hours, send a second SMS: 'Sleep debt at <X> hours. Consider an early night.' Otherwise stay quiet.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning text logging sleep and a follow-up only when your rolling sleep debt is high.
automations.customization: Change the target hours, the debt threshold, or add a third nightly bedtime ping.

automations.name: PostgreSQL Slow Query Spotter
automations.prompt: Run a bash psql command against the database using $DATABASE_URL to query pg_stat_statements ordered by mean_exec_time desc limit 20, filtered to calls > 50 and mean_exec_time > 500. Diff results against /home/workspace/Ops/pg-slow-baseline.csv. For any queryid not in the baseline whose mean_exec_time is above 1000ms, POST a Slack alert to $SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL with the queryid, mean_exec_time, calls, and the first 200 chars of the query text. Update the baseline file at the end of each run.
automations.expectedOutput: A Slack ping only when a newly slow Postgres query crosses one second average execution time.
automations.customization: Adjust the time and call thresholds, narrow to specific schemas, or switch delivery to email.

automations.name: Cold Outreach Reply Rate Tracker
automations.prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search the 'Sent' label for messages with subject prefix 'Outreach:' from the last 14 days. For each, check whether a reply exists in the same thread. Compute total_sent, total_replied, and reply_rate. Read /home/workspace/Projects/outreach/campaigns.yml to attribute each sent message to a campaign based on subject tag (e.g. [c-spring]). Append weekly metrics per campaign to /home/workspace/Projects/outreach/reply-rates.csv. Email a Friday summary via send_email_to_user with subject 'Outreach reply rates, <overall>%' as a table.
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday email with reply rates per cold-outreach campaign over the last two weeks.
automations.customization: Change the subject tag scheme, the lookback window, or split by send weekday.

automations.name: Logfire Error Spike Watcher
automations.prompt: Run a bash curl against the Logfire query API using $LOGFIRE_READ_TOKEN to count error-level spans in the last 10 minutes across services listed in /home/workspace/Ops/logfire-services.yml. Read the previous 10 minute baseline from /home/workspace/Ops/logfire-baseline.json. If the current count exceeds max(20, baseline * 3), send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user: 'Error spike in <service>: <count> errors, baseline <baseline>'. Always update the rolling baseline as an exponential moving average.
automations.expectedOutput: An SMS only when a tracked service crosses an error spike threshold relative to its rolling baseline.
automations.customization: Tune the spike multiplier, the floor, or which services are watched.

automations.name: Indie Hacker Comp Scan
automations.prompt: Read /home/workspace/Projects/competitive/comps.yml, a list of competitor product names, X handles, and changelog URLs. For each, run web_search for '<product> changelog OR release' restricted to the last week, and run x_search for posts from the X handle in the same window. Summarize each competitor in 3 bullets (shipped, repositioned, claims). Email a Monday brief via send_email_to_user with subject 'Comp scan, week of <date>' as a markdown report grouped per product.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email summarizing what each tracked competitor shipped or said publicly in the last 7 days.
automations.customization: Add or remove competitors, change the time window, or weight by changelog vs social signals.

automations.name: AWS Bill Anomaly Alert
automations.prompt: Run a bash aws-cli call: 'aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=<7daysago>,End=<today> --granularity DAILY --metrics UnblendedCost --group-by Type=DIMENSION,Key=SERVICE'. Parse JSON for per-service daily cost. Compute each service's 7 day average and yesterday's cost. Flag any service where yesterday is greater than max(2.0 dollars, 1.6 * 7 day average). Append daily totals to /home/workspace/Finance/aws-daily.csv. Email a flagged-services report via send_email_to_user with subject 'AWS spend anomaly, <count> services' only when at least one service flags.
automations.expectedOutput: A morning email only on days when AWS spend for a service jumps well above its weekly baseline.
automations.customization: Tune the multiplier, set a per-service floor, or include linked-account breakdowns.

automations.name: The Open PR Bottleneck Map
automations.prompt: For each repo in /home/workspace/Projects/oss/repos.txt, use the gh CLI to list open PRs with reviewers, requested reviewers, latest review state, and CI conclusion. Categorize each PR into exactly one bottleneck: 'waiting on author' (changes requested, no new push), 'waiting on review' (no review yet, CI green), 'CI broken', or 'merge conflict'. Build a markdown report grouping PRs by category across all repos with PR title, repo, author, and age in days. Email via send_email_to_user with subject 'PR bottlenecks: <total> open'.
automations.expectedOutput: A twice-weekly email mapping every open PR to the specific reason it is not merging.
automations.customization: Add labels to filter on, exclude bots, or send only one category (e.g. 'CI broken') as SMS.

automations.name: Tomorrow's Outfit Weather Check
automations.prompt:
You are running an evening outfit check for tomorrow.

1. Call web_search with the query: 'hourly forecast Salmon Idaho tomorrow' (one query, latest results).
2. From the results, pull: morning low, afternoon high, precipitation chance, wind, and any advisories.
3. Translate the forecast into a 2 sentence outfit suggestion. Mention layers, footwear, and whether to grab a coat or a hat. Be concrete (for example: 'Fleece plus shell, waterproof boots').
4. Send the result via send_sms_to_user. Keep it under 320 characters total. No greetings, no signoff.

Do not include any links. Do not echo the raw forecast numbers unless they matter for the outfit call.
automations.expectedOutput: A short SMS at 9pm with tomorrow's forecast translated into a 2 sentence outfit call.
automations.customization: Change the location in the search query, the send time, or swap SMS for Telegram.

automations.name: The Lunch Break Forcer
automations.prompt:
You are running the lunch break enforcer.

1. Call use_app_google_calendar with tool 'google-calendar-list-events' on the primary calendar for the window noon to 2pm local time today.
2. Find the longest contiguous free gap of at least 30 minutes in that window.
3. If a gap exists, send an SMS naming the exact start and end time of the gap and one specific lunch action (for example: 'Walk to the kitchen, eat the leftovers, no laptop').
4. If the entire window is booked, send an SMS that names the back to back meetings and suggests the user block 30 minutes after the last one.

Use send_sms_to_user. One message only. No emojis.
automations.expectedOutput: An SMS naming a specific lunch window or flagging an overbooked midday.
automations.customization: Adjust the lunch window, the minimum gap length, or the message style.

automations.name: School Pickup Window Alert
automations.prompt:
You are running the school pickup window alert.

1. Call use_app_google_calendar with tool 'google-calendar-list-events' on the primary calendar from now until 5pm today.
2. Call web_search for 'traffic conditions [city] afternoon' replacing [city] with the user's stored city if known, otherwise skip this step.
3. Estimate the latest moment the user should leave the house to reach the school by 3:15pm. Assume a 15 minute drive unless traffic results say otherwise.
4. Send an SMS with: the leave-by time, any calendar conflicts in that window, and one short note about traffic if relevant.

Use send_sms_to_user. Keep it under 280 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: An SMS at 2pm naming the leave-by time and any conflict with that window.
automations.customization: Change the pickup time, the assumed drive time, or the city in the traffic query.

automations.name: Sunday Meal Prep Scout
automations.prompt:
You are planning the user's meal prep for the coming week.

1. Call use_app_google_calendar to list events Monday through Friday this week.
2. Identify which weeknights have evening commitments after 5pm. Those nights need fast or pre-made dinners.
3. Call web_search for 'one pan dinner recipes 30 minutes' and pick 3 distinct results that are not duplicates of the last 4 weeks.
4. Build a meal plan: 2 prep-ahead lunches, 5 dinners (matched to the calendar), and one grocery list grouped by produce, protein, pantry.
5. Send via send_email_to_user with the subject 'Meal Plan: Week of [Monday date]'. Format the body in plain markdown with sections.
automations.expectedOutput: A Sunday morning email with a weekday meal plan matched to the user's calendar plus a grouped grocery list.
automations.customization: Swap the recipe style, change dietary constraints in the search query, or move the send day.

automations.name: Library Book Due Date Tracker
automations.prompt:
You are checking the user's library checkout log.

1. Call read_file on '/home/workspace/Documents/library-checkouts.md'. If the file does not exist, send an email explaining the user needs to create that file with a markdown table of title, due date, renewals used.
2. Parse each row. Flag any book due within 4 days as URGENT, due within 10 days as SOON, and overdue as LATE.
3. For each LATE or URGENT row, suggest one of: renew, return today, finish tonight.
4. Send via send_email_to_user with the subject 'Library: X urgent, Y soon, Z late'.

If the table is empty, send a one line email saying the shelf is clear.
automations.expectedOutput: A Wednesday email listing books due soon with a one word action for each.
automations.customization: Change the file path, the urgency thresholds, or the day of the week.

automations.name: The Quiet Hour Calendar Guard
automations.prompt:
You are protecting the user's morning quiet hour, defined as 8am to 9am local time.

1. Call use_app_google_calendar to list events for today between 8am and 9am.
2. If any event lands inside that window, write a short note for each: title, organizer, whether it looks declinable (recurring team standup vs. one-off external).
3. If the window is clear, send a one line email: 'Quiet hour clear. Use it.'
4. If the window is taken, list each conflict and suggest a specific reschedule slot from the rest of the morning that is open.

Use send_email_to_user. Subject: 'Quiet hour status'.
automations.expectedOutput: A 6am email confirming the 8 to 9 block is clear, or listing every conflict with a suggested move.
automations.customization: Change the quiet hour window, the days it runs, or the delivery channel.

automations.name: Birthday Card Address Audit
automations.prompt:
You are checking the user's birthday list for the coming 3 weeks.

1. Call read_file on '/home/workspace/Documents/birthdays.md'. Expect a markdown table with name, date (MM-DD), relationship, address known yes/no.
2. For each birthday falling in the next 21 days, compute the mail-by date as 5 calendar days before the birthday.
3. Group results into 3 buckets: 'Card this week', 'Card next week', 'Card week after'.
4. For anyone with address known = no, add them to a 'Need address' bucket.
5. Send via send_email_to_user. Subject: 'Birthday cards: [N] this week'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Monday email grouping upcoming birthdays into mail-by buckets plus a missing-address list.
automations.customization: Change the lead time, the file path, or the bucket structure.

automations.name: The Friday Pantry Audit
automations.prompt:
You are running the Friday pantry audit.

1. Call read_file on '/home/workspace/Documents/pantry.md'. Expect a markdown list with item, quantity, last restocked date.
2. Flag items with quantity 'low' or last restocked over 90 days ago.
3. Identify items that pair into a meal (rice plus a protein plus a vegetable). Suggest 2 meals the user could make this weekend without shopping.
4. Build a shopping list of low items grouped by produce, dairy, dry goods.
5. Send via send_email_to_user with subject 'Pantry: [N] low, [M] weekend meals possible'.
automations.expectedOutput: A Friday evening email with a shopping list, low item flags, and 2 weekend meal options from existing stock.
automations.customization: Change the file format, the staleness threshold, or the meal style.

automations.name: Trash Day Night-Before Ping
automations.prompt:
You are reminding the user about trash pickup tomorrow.

1. Determine which day of the week tomorrow is.
2. Call web_search for 'holiday trash pickup schedule [city] this week' replacing [city] with the user's city if known. If no holiday delay is found, assume the regular schedule.
3. If tomorrow is a regular pickup day, send an SMS: 'Trash out tonight. [Recycling/Compost] too if applicable.'
4. If a holiday delay shifted the schedule, send an SMS naming the new pickup day.

Use send_sms_to_user. One message. No emojis. Under 200 characters.
automations.expectedOutput: An 8pm text the night before pickup with a one line reminder and any holiday shift.
